name: Carnegie AI Safety Initiative
description: CASI seeks to spark interest in and facilitate research about AI safety and alignment.

name: Effective Altruism CMU
description: Do you want to make a positive impact with your career? We focus on how you can choose your career to have the *greatest* impact whether by focusing on alleviating global poverty, trying to prevent future pandemics, trying to prevent nuclear war, and lots more. Every semester we run a seminar that outlines all of the areas in which you can have the most impact with your career. The seminar is a bit of a time commitment, taking 3-4 hours per week for eight weeks. There will be weekly meetings and reading assignments. Food will be provided at no cost to you. We have both a general seminar on the aforementioned topic, as well as cause-specific seminars (AI safety, particularly).  Our group also runs weekly dinners and various different socials. We are part of a global movement with groups at many top university. We frequently collaborate with Stanford, MIT, and Cambridge. We welcome both undergrads and graduate students.Join our Discord: https://discord.gg/N4qfs2Tevb  We were formerly known as "Strategic Social Impact" @ CMU

name: 180 Degrees Consulting CMU Branch
description: 180 Degrees Consulting is the world's largest consultancy for nonprofits and socially focused organizations. Our mission is to ensure that non-profits and social enterprises committed to education, health, and poverty alleviation can reach their full potential by meeting their demand for very affordable, high-quality strategic and operational assistance, and in so doing developing the next generation of social impact leaders.

name: AB Tech
description: The Activities Board Technical Committee, commonly known as AB Tech, is the student organization charged with handling the technical production of Carnegie Mellon-sponsored events, including all events organized by the Activities Board. AB Tech provides high-quality, professional-grade entertainment production services, including sound reinforcement, lighting design, and event management at heavily subsidized rates to the Carnegie Mellon University community. Clients include student groups, university faculty and administration, and various other organizations.AB Tech provides technical support in three core areas:- Sound Reinforcement- Lighting Design- Production Consulting and Event ManagementIn addition to these three areas, we coordinate associated needs and services such as multi-phase/high-load power, rigging and trussing, staging rentals, and backline rentals.

name: AC Mellon Men's Soccer Team
description: Our ambition is to foster a fun competitive culture and win at the highest soccer club levels. We find success in our commitment to the club  and our positive culture.

name: ACM@CMU
description: The mission of this organization is to be the leading premier organization for computing professionals at Carnegie Mellon. We are dedicated to promoting interdisciplinary computing and enabling professional development on campus.

name: Activities Board
description: To provide quality programming for the CMU student body!

name: acts2fellowship
description: Acts2Fellowship (A2F) is a Christian fellowship for students at CMU. We're a group of students and mentors who love Jesus. We do life together, go on trips, study together, serve the community and above all, investigate the biggest questions of life and grow in our faith.

name: Adam Smith Society
description: The Adam Smith Society is a community of business school students and alumni who believe that business, entrepreneurship, and commerce are wellsprings that keep this country vibrant, creative, prosperous, and free.Our members are dedicated to exploring the links among the economy, government, and society through rigorous debate and discussion.The Society aims to build an influential network of future business leaders dedicated to preserving and strengthening the free-market economic system.In concert with dedicated MBA student leaders, the national office of the Adam Smith Society underwrites and helps to coordinate a series of exclusive on-campus events that give MBA students access to prominent business leaders, academics, journalists, and public officials. Speakers discuss their experiences and offer insights about the connections between successful businesses, the maintenance of our nation’s free institutions, and a robust civil society. The Adam Smith Society also convenes national and regional programming for student members and alumni.  We hope to inspire business students to think about their own responsibility in seeing our economic system endure.

name: African Business Collective (ABC)
description: To increase interest in Africa by disseminating knowledge on the business landscape, policy, economic development and culture

name: African Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of the African Graduate Student Association is to provide a supportive and welcomingenvironment for graduate students of African descent at Carnegie Mellon University. Throughsocial, cultural, and professional development events throughout the academic year, we aim toenable our members to become advocates on issues pertaining to development in Africa. Our hopeis to engage with the CMU community through our events to create awareness of the cultural andsocio-economic riches of the African Continent.

name: Agape Christian Ministry
description: Agapé Christian Ministry seeks to promote spiritual life and provide a faith-based community for the student body at Carnegie Mellon University. It also aids in Christian maturity, growth in interpersonal relationships, and leadership opportunities. Its programs are open to any undergraduate, graduate or professional student.

name: Alexander Hamilton Society
description: The Carnegie Mellon University Chapter of the Alexander Hamilton Society is a student-run organization that is a part of a larger organization of students, faculty, national policy leaders, and captains of finance and industry. The society seeks to develop the next generation of American leaders, initially through discussions and forums on campus, then to intellectual and professional development that will launch Hamiltonians into careers of public service, academia, and business.

name: All University Orchestra
description: All University Orchestra is a community of CMU undergrads, graduate students, staff, and faculty who love to make music. Everyone is welcome to participate!We have three ensembles: Symphony Orchestra (a full-scale symphony orchestra), AUO Chamber Orchestra (formerly String Theory), and Flutonium (a flute choir). Each puts on one concert per semester, available free of charge to members of the CMU community and the public.We have an informal placement audition process to ensure we place everybody into an appropriate ensemble and achieve proper balance across parts. No need to stress about these - anybody who wants to can participate.Be sure to check out our website, join our Discord, like our Facebook page, and follow our Instagram (we follow back)!

name: Alpha Chi Omega
description: Welcome to Carnegie Mellon University’s Kappa Nu.Chapter of Alpha Chi Omega.We are a group of passionate, down to Earth women with a strong bond of sisterhoodand an even stronger desire to change the world.Real. Strong. Women.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta
description: Alpha Epsilon Delta is the national health preprofessional honor society founded in 2015 at Carnegie Mellon University. We are dedicated to the encouragement and recognition of excellence in preprofessional health scholarship. This co-ed fraternity welcomes students engaged in the pursuit of a professional development, provides a forum for students with common interests, and extends a program of service to benefit the university as well as the community. AED functions both as an honor society and a service organization. AED helps guide students into being physicians of integrity with lifelong commitment to guiding principles of philanthropy, and education through mentoring, fellowship, service, and formal training.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi Professional Business Fraternity
description: Alpha Kappa Psi is the nation's oldest and largest co-ed professional fraternity open to all majors and interests. Here at Carnegie Mellon, the Omega Psi chapter has established itself as a leading student organization, consistently attracting the best and brightest minds on campus.

name: Alpha Omega Campus Ministry
description: Alpha Omega is a campus ministry in the great city of Pittsburgh helping students to know more about God and fall in love with Him. We are a community of believers committed to Jesus and his ways and our greatest desire is to help others to know Him!

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description: To develop the chapter as an essential part of the CMU and Scouting communities, make diversity and inclusion a priority in our organization, and to best serve Pittsburgh and beyond.

name: Alpine Racing & Freestyle Team
description: Ski Team promotes the sports of ski racing, freestyle skiing and snowboarding on campus. Ski Team provides an opportunity for students who enjoy these sports to compete in the Allegheny Collegiate Ski and Snowboard Conference, a division of the United States Collegiate Ski and Snowboard Association. Ski Team provides resources and support for all competitors to expand their skills and have a safe and enjoyable race experience.

name: American Association for Aerosol Research
description: The American Association for Aerosol Research (AAAR) describes itself as “a nonprofitprofessional organization for scientists and engineers who wish to promote and communicatetechnical advances in the field of aerosol research.” The Carnegie Mellon University studentchapter of AAAR provides a link between students at CMU and the larger AAAR community.Our student chapter also aims to:1. Serve the intellectual and professional interests of students pursuing a career in the fieldof aerosol research2. Share these interests with the broader Carnegie Mellon University communityWe work towards these objectives in many ways, including, but not limited to:- Engaging in educational outreach (for example, presenting scientific demos to localchildren through CMU’s Leonard Gelfand Center for Service Learning and Outreach)- Organizing the Annual Donora Lecture, in which an invited speaker from the field ofaerosol research comes to campus, gives a talk, and engages with students and facultyassociated with our chapter.- Building a strong sense of community among members through summer barbecues andbiweekly coffee/tea hours.

name: American Indigenous Science and Engineering Society
description: Chapters are formed to advance the mission of American Indigenous Science and Engineering Society (AISES) at colleges and universities with Indigenous students in attendance and/or official support services for these students. The objective is to support the success of Native American, Alaska Native, Native Hawaiian, and Pacific Islander students in their intellectual, academic, social, and professional pursuits in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) and related disciplines.

name: American Institute of Architecture Students
description: The mission of The American Institute of Architecture Students shall be to promote excellence in architectural education, training, and practice; to foster an appreciation of architecture and related disciplines; to enrich communities in a spirit of collaboration; and to organize students and combine their efforts to advance the art and science of architecture.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
description: To empower our members by providing a diverse and inclusive support system which includes students, alumni, faculty, and industry professionals. We provide social, academic, and professional support as well as opportunities to hear from different leading civil and environmental engineering companies about the work that they are doing to shape our future.

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: ASME's mission is to serve the Carnegie Mellon and Pittsburgh Communities by advancing and applying engineering knowledge for improving the quality of life. We also seek to exemplify the excitement of Engineering.

name: Apex
description: Apex began in 2012 when a group of friends living in the Rez on Fifth had the crazy idea of starting a buggy team. Now in its second decade, Apex remains dedicated to the principles that motivated its formation: teamwork, passion, innovation, and excitement. As an open source team, Apex aims to challenge the field of buggy by not living up to the status quo set by other organizations. We want to see the sport succeed through collaboration within the buggy community! Apex is a team of pushers, drivers, and mechanics. But first and foremost, we are a group of friends who have come together to support and challenge each other, share our excitement of buggy, and have fun along the way. So if you are interested in buggy and want to learn more, come to our events!

name: Architectural Crafts Collective
description: To create a platform for students interested in architecture and design to collaborate and expand their knowledge on fabrication, design, and creative skills with an emphasis on traditional crafts and techniques

name: Arm Wrestling Club
description: AWC intends to teach others about the techniques of safe arm wrestling and promote strength/exercise across campus. Members will learn the vital importance of being stronger. AWC embraces a long standing motto: “Make your way up the food chain” to symbolize one’s journey in becoming the strongest version of themselves.

name: Artist Alley Club
description: In anime and comic conventions, there is a section for independent artists to sell their work. This is called the "artist alley". Artist Alley Club is a community of artists dedicated to helping each other with activities related to participating in artist alley, like creating original and fan art and printing and making merchandise of it. We also act as a community for artists who are generally part of the artist alley subculture: fan-artists and people who make cartoon-, video game-, or anime-inspired art.

name: Asian Christian Fellowship
description: The Asian Christian Fellowship seeks to carry out the Great Commission of our Lord Jesus Christ to make disciples of all nations by proclaiming the gospel, providing fellowship for edification, nur- turing Christians in spiritual growth, and building up Christian leaders.

name: Asian Students Association
description: The Asian Students Association seeks to empower and connect the Asian American and Pacific Islander community at Carnegie Mellon University.

name: Association of Latino Professionals For America
description: The purpose of this organization is to create a community here at Carnegie Mellon University where Latino students are empowered to contribute to one another's growth, development, and successes, both personally while they are here on campus and professionally as they look to step into the workforce.

name: Astronomy Club
description: Founded in 1967, we are a diverse group of Carnegie Mellon undergrads, graduate students, and community members who are astronomy and space enthusiasts. We meet throughout the fall and spring semesters, discuss astronomy, take trips to observatories and museums, and go observing whenever we get the chance (admittedly infrequent in cloudy Pittsburgh).

name: Automotive Mechanic Club
description: Our club is a haven for budding engineers with a passion for automotive craftsmanship. We unite students with a shared love for cars and mechanics, fostering a community that transcends textbooks. Through hands-on workshops, collaborative projects, and expert guest lectures, we empower members to deepen their understanding of automotive engineering. Our mission is to fuel curiosity, spark innovation, and rev up practical skills. Join us in navigating the intricate world of automotive mechanics, where every turn of the wrench brings us closer to mastery. Embrace the grease, the grit, and the glory of engineering on four wheels! We hope to make our CMU education coincide with teaching life skill, being how to work on cars

name: Awareness of Roots in Chinese Culture
description: Awareness of Roots in Chinese Culture (ARCC) is a CMU organisation which serves to promote Chinese culture on campus. Our goal is to increase awareness of Chinese culture on the CMU campus by sponsoring large open events, such as the Mid-Autumn Festival, Chinese New Year and Dumpling Nights, along with organizing annual cultural performances involving traditional dance, martial arts, and musical performances. We hope that you will join us at our events and learn more about Chinese culture!

name: Badminton Club
description: The goal of this organization is to cultivate an increased interest in badminton at CMU through providing badminton enthusiasts a safe community to develop their skills. With opportunities to train weekly and compete in tournaments, Badminton Club aims to foster a motivating and competitive atmosphere for its members, encouraging every athlete to continue their journey with badminton.

name: Ballroom Dance Club
description: The CMU Ballroom Dance Club's goal is to create a setting for learning social and competitive ballroom dancing in a fun and relaxed environment. Our club's members range from those with two left feet (and sometimes more) to veteran dancers with years of experience. We welcome anyone interested in learning and having fun, including Pittsburgh area undergraduate students, graduate students, and professionals. If you don't have a partner, no worries! We welcome singles and couples.

name: Bangladeshi Student Organization
description: The Bangladeshi Student Organization (BSO) at Carnegie Mellon University aims to provide a home for students and faculty of Bengali descent. Our mission is to foster a supportive environment where Bengali students can celebrate and stay connected to their cultural heritage. We are equally committed to building an inclusive community that welcomes all CMU students who are interested in learning about Bangladeshi culture, traditions, and perspectives.

name: Be The Match
description: Our goal is to raise awareness and money for the Be The Match charity. This charity gets people on a registry for donating their hematopoietic stem cells, which can save lives through transplants to those with blood cancer and other diseases.

name: Bhakti Yoga Club
description: To create a welcoming and inclusive community space grounded in the principles of bhakti yoga, the yoga of devotion and loving service. We aim to help students relieve stress through various meditation techniques, including music meditation, while offering opportunities for spiritual growth, connection, and cultural exchange.

name: Bhangra in the Burgh
description: Bhangra in the Burgh's mission is to promote Indian and more specifically Punjabi culture within the Pittsburgh community through our annual premier dance competition. We also hope to give back to the Pittsburgh community by donating all proceeds to a charity of our choice every year.

name: Big Straw Magazine
description: The Asian-interest publication that started over a cup of bubble tea.Big Straw is a student-run Asian-interest publication that celebrates the creative talent andintellectual depth of the Asian and Asian American community at Carnegie Mellon University byproviding students with an outlet for artistic expression, cultural identity, and thought. BigStraw was founded with the goal to cultivate and spread ideas relevant to Asians and AsianAmericans.

name: Biomedical Engineering Society, Carnegie Mellon Chapter
description: The objective of this chapter shall be to promote the profession of Biomedical Engineering through organized study, research, social, and industry immersed activities. These activities will work to further the dissemination of information on the topic and profession of biomedical engineering, as well as its interaction with the other science disciplines.  The chapter shall promote membership in the BMES by facilitating community within the student body and faculty through the ideas, purposes and objectives of BMES.

name: Birding@CMU
description: We hope to create a love for birds and conservation at CMU and beyond.

name: Black Business Association Club
description: The Black Business Association (BBA) is dedicated to the academic, professional, and personal development of its members. The organization supports the recruitment of diverse students to the Tepper School of Business and welcomes all Carnegie Mellon University students who support its objectives.

name: Black Graduate Students Organization
description: e Black Graduate Student Organization (BGSO) is a support group for graduate students of color as they pursue academic excellence on the campus of Carnegie Mellon University. Throughout the academic year, BGSO provides graduate students with cultural, academic, and professional support. BGSO inspires its members to be active leaders in both the CMU and Pittsburgh communities. BGSO members represent various ethnic backgrounds and academic disciplines across the CMU campus.

name: Black Latino Business Association
description: The Black Latino Business Association strives to create a community for undergraduate students ofdiverse ethnic backgrounds (groups which are currently underrepresented in the field of business) to support one another in their academic and professional aspirations.

name: Block Center for Technology and Society
description: Block Center is a campus wide research center that serve the entire CMU campus/community to explore the intersections of technological innovation, public policy, and society.

name: Brewmeisters Club
description: It is the mission of this organization to relentlessly pursue and amass as much knowledge and experience of the brewing and tasting of world-class beer as is humanly possible.

name: Bridge Club
description: Our mission is to learn and master contract bridge, which is a card game played by millions of people. We have regular club play and also participate in tournaments.

name: Business & Technology Club
description: The Business and Technology Club (B&T) is a professional club focused on exposing members to career opportunities in technology functions and industries. Our goal is to develop future industry leaders by engaging with technology experts, exploring the impact of emerging technologies, facilitating business case competitions, and networking with leading professionals at the most innovative companies in the world.B&T is the primary club contact for all recruiting, case competitions, treks, and other events with technology, e-commerce, media, and entertainment companies at Tepper. The Club sponsors a variety of exclusive events, as well as events with other clubs and cross-campus schools, such as the Welcome Weekend, to effectively prepare members, and to expose our members to new and developing technologies.

name: C#
description: C# is an organization which develops programs that contribute to the growth of a supportive musical community: a place where members are encouraged to unwind from academics by challenging themselves as singers, performers, and leaders. This community is home to the C Sharp Singers, our competitive a cappella group, and the D Flat Singers, our serious choir group, and C++, our open branch focused on community outreach and engagement. Each group is comprised of students dedicated to growing as musicians and fostering the musical community at CMU.

name: Camp Kesem Carnegie Mellon
description: Camp Kesem is a camp dedicated to helping kids move through and beyond their parent'scancer.  Camp Kesem is a national non-profit organization that was started at Stanford University in 2000.  Since then Camp Kesem has started 86 chapters and has helped bring a free week of summer camp to more than 6000 kids each year. Camp Kesem gives kids a week to be kids and help them meet others who can support them and relate to what they have been through.  It also gives parents a week off so they don't have to worry about their kids.  Camp Kesem brings families together and creates a community of support among them.  Camp Kesem also helps connect the community to the cause and gives everyone a chance to contribute and make a difference.  Kesem means magic in Hebrew and it is said that the kids and counselors make magic at camp.  Here at Carnegie Mellon University we look for students who are dedicated, passionate, and eager to get involved. Camp Kesem welcomes any and all members of the CK community, and allow students to serve under various officer positions. CK alumni and community partners may only serve as associates.

name: Career & Professional Development Center
description: The Career and Professional Development Center empowers students and alumni to optimize their professional and life potential through career exploration, experiential learning, and connections with employers and opportunities.

name: Carnegie Involvement Association
description: Founded in 1970, CIA was the first independent (non-Greek) organization to win Sweepstakes, or Buggy. Open to all members of the student body, CIA's objective is to strike the right balance between performing well and having fun.

name: Carnegie Mellon Artificial Intelligence Club
description: The Carnegie Mellon Artificial Intelligence Club (CMUAI) is committed to fostering a dynamic community for AI enthusiasts. Through seminars, hackathons, and collaborations, we aim to amplify understanding, promote its application in daily life, and ensure CMU remains at the AI forefront.

name: Carnegie Mellon Business Association
description: The mission of CMBA is to provide students with the opportunity to learn about and engage with various aspects of the business world. From industry panels to LinkedIn workshops, we aim to help our peers hone skills applicable to interviewing, searching for jobs, and presenting well. CMBA also hosts three industry programs where participants learn about their respective field through a prepared curriculum, interactive discussions, and internal competitions.

name: Carnegie Mellon Club XC
description: To provide a space for a team and community for passionate endurance runners to compete interscholasitically and train together.

name: Carnegie Mellon College Progressives
description: The CMCP’s mission is to build an educational foundation for leftist theory and practice on CMU’s campus. We advocate for and help organize workers on and off campus, create a space to study and discuss relevant leftist literature, and analyze local politics through a socialist lens; with a focus on class, race, and LGBTQ+ issues, imperialism and colonialism, the labor movement, and more.

name: Carnegie Mellon College Republicans
description: The purpose of the Carnegie Mellon College Republicans is to raise awareness for Republican and conservative values within Carnegie Mellon University. Our overall objective is to promote open discourse and critical thinking regarding politics by providing a platform which allows insight into conservative values through structured discussion.

name: Carnegie Mellon Colombian Student Organization
description: Promoting Colombian heritage and providing resources for student success and community involvement.

name: Carnegie Mellon Dance Team
description: Our mission is to improve CMU school spirit with performances at home sporting events

name: Carnegie Mellon Data Science Club
description: The purpose of this organization is to serve as a platform for CMU students to pursue their interests in data science in a setting that is supportive, collaborative, and competitive.

name: Students Using Data for Social-Good
description: We aim to connect socially conscious students from all CMU schools to non-profits where they can apply their technical and communication skills to meaningful data management and analysis projects.

name: Carnegie Mellon Embedded Systems Club
description: The Embedded Systems Club provides Carnegie Mellon students with hands-on experience in embedded systems through projects, skill-building, participating in embedded systems competitions, and industry connections.

name: Carnegie Mellon Graduate Consulting Club
description: The main purpose of The Carnegie Mellon Graduate Consulting Club is to inform graduate students of opportunities within the management consulting industry, help prepare students for entering the industry, help students connect with professionals in the management consulting space, help groom students for successful careers in management consulting, and facilitate communication between our group and consulting firms

name: Carnegie Mellon Informatics and Mathematics Competition Organizing Team
description: CMIMC's mission is to unite a group of passionate students to organize math and computer science  competitions for high schoolers, and use our platform for good.

name: Carnegie Mellon Men's Lacrosse
description: Be the greatest of all time

name: Carnegie Mellon Mixed Martial Arts
description: The Carnegie Mellon Mixed Martial Arts Club (CMU MMAC) aims to provide students the opportunity to learn and practice Mixed Martial Arts (MMA) in a safe setting. Regardless of experience or age, CMU MMAC wants to help students to gain and hone their skills for self-defense or for competition, while developing self-discipline and improved mental and physical well-being.

name: Carnegie Mellon Philosophical Society
description: The CMU Philosophical Society seeks to promote philosophy as a field of study to the student body at Carnegie Mellon. The Philosophical Society will house programs designed to enhance one's analysis of philosophical literature, ability to create and share arguments, and capacity to teach other students in the field.

name: Carnegie Mellon Racing
description: As an organization, we aim to be at the forefront of electric vehicle racecar technology. We provide students with opportunities for technical and management experience on a large engineering project.  We strive to cultivate excellent engineers, leaders, and problem solvers. Our team is accessible to all people of different experience levels, backgrounds, and areas of study.

name: Carnegie Mellon Racquetball Club
description: The Carnegie Mellon University racquetball club is open to all students, faculty, and staff. Skill levels range from beginners to advanced players who have competed at the Pro level.

name: Carnegie Mellon Rocket Command
description: Our mission is to introduce students to rocketry through hands-on experience building both personal rockets and a team rocket for collegiate competitions. Members will be involved in the design, fabrication, and testing of all components, including control surfaces, parachute deployment, payloads, electronics, and more.

name: Carnegie Mellon Running Club
description: Our mission is to create a community of people who can bond through the sport of running. We hope to provide members with the information and support needed to meet their individual fitness goals.

name: Carnegie Mellon Solar Racing
description: Carnegie Mellon Solar Racing is a competitive solar racing team looking to promote the use of sustainable energy, namely solar-powered energy, and its use in society. The organization acts as an interdisciplinary space for students to hone their soft skills, tackle complex problems, and learn about building and maintaining a boat. We aim to provide our members with networking opportunities and engineering experience that will go on to serve them in their professional careers.CMSR designs, constructs, and races solar-powered carbon-fiber boats. Each boat is around 6 meters long and is powered solely by solar energy. CMSR splits has 4 primary teams that design different parts of the boat: Power, Propulsion, Hull, and Optimization. We are welcome all students interested in solar power, working on hands-on engineering projects, or who love just boats.

name: Carnegie Mellon Sports Analytics Club
description: The primary purpose of this club shall be to encourage teamwork, integrity, anddevelopment of the skills and knowledge needed to be successful in the sports analyticsfield.

name: Carnegie Mellon Tricking Club
description: CMU Tricking will teach the fundamentals of tricking to new members of all skill levels in a safe, supportive environment, in order to spread interest in this emerging sport. Follow @cmutricking on instagram for more information!

name: Carnegie Mellon University Club Squash
description: Carnegie Mellon University club squash is a co-ed, all-levels team for anyone interested in playing squash. As a student-run club, we host 3-4 optional practices per week and occasional squash workshops for each level. We also compete against different club squash teams outside of Carnegie Mellon University and attend tournaments including the National Collegiate Club Team Championships hosted by the college squash association.

name: Carnegie Mellon University Club Swimming
description: CMU Club swimming is a fun way to keep in shape and be part of a great group on campus. We have practices 3 times per week, attend 2-4 meets per semester against other schools, and have various social events. We are open to people of all swimming levels, ranging from those that just want to stay shape to those that want to be competitive at meets.Practices are Tuesdays 7-9pm, Wednesdays 9-11pm, and Sundays 4-5:30pm. Practices are optional and you can show up or leave whenever convenient. We will provide a swim set and equipment to use during practice.INSTAGRAM: @cmuclubswimming

name: Carnegie Mellon University Cricket Club
description: The CMU Cricket club is for all cricket lovers. We promote the sport for one and all, be it current students, alumni, faculty to enjoy and learn the game of cricket at CMU. The mission of the club is not just to promote the sport itself, but also build future leaders and develop team work through this sport.

name: Carnegie Mellon University Figure Skating Club
description: To promote figure skating at CMU and build a community of figure skaters

name: Carnegie Mellon University Global Medical Brigades
description: CMU GMB is a chapter of the national organization Global Brigades. Global Brigades is a global health and sustainable development organization that aims to empower rural communities in Guatemala, Panama, Honduras, and Ghana through student brigades. CMU GMB's mission is to aid in this initiative by providing access to medical care and creating sustainable infrastructures in underserved communities abroad. Our organization focuses on holistic health care development of the rural areas we visit. For half of our brigade, we facilitate free mobile clinics. With a team of recruited students and licensed medical professionals, we provide basic and essential health care to underserved communities. A large aspect of clinics also targets long-term health problems by hosting educational talks about dietary health and water sanitation. Educational talks about dental hygiene are also held with the children that visit the clinic. The other half of our brigade consists of public health related projects that include educational presentations and infrastructural projects like laying concrete floors and building sanitation stations. Global Brigades also offers Telebrigades, where we meet with local health care workers to learn about the country's healthcare system and gain virtual shadowing experience. We also believe that leaving an impact in our local community should be just as much part of our mission as our global community. Therefore we will be taking further steps to increase our impact in our local community.  Our organization believes that a multifaceted approach that targets health problems with immediate and long term relief can help these communities reach a better standard of living.

name: Carnegie Mellon University Math Club
description: The purpose of CMUMC shall be to promote themathematical growth of individuals within CMU with a focus on the undergraduate experience. We aim to hold social and informational events that will promote the holistic and professional goals of students interested in mathematics.

name: Carnegie Mellon University Model United Nations
description: The purpose of the Carnegie Mellon University Model United Nations Team is to encourage and promote diplomacy, dialogue, and understanding of different cultures and ideas through the provision of academic and social events relevant to international cooperation, understanding, and education

name: Carnegie Mellon University Poker Club
description: The Undergraduate Poker Club strives to be a hub for the poker community at Carnegie Mellon University to meet, learn, and play. We aim to engage all manner of poker players with a wide array of events that include everything from charity tournaments and interactive workshops to guest speakers and networking opportunities. Outside of CMU, we seek to build lasting connections with trading firms and other companies that are interested in students with the strategic and analytical skills developed through poker.

name: Carnegie Mellon University 岸韵 Chinese Dance Club (ACDC)
Carnegie Mellon Young Americans for Liberty
Carnegie Tech Radio Club
description: The radio club is an organization of people interested in all the activities amateur radio has to offer. Our membership consists of undergraduates, graduate students, faculty, and staff of the CMU community. Among the club members there is a wide variety of interests such as public service events, satellite communications, DXing (talking to other amateurs around the world), computers and networks, amateur television, and emergency drills. Additionally, the club is actively involved in providing course status and emergency communications for all buggy free-rolls as well as on race day. We have meetings regularly in the shack on the 4th floor of Hamerschlag Hall, so please stop by and check us out. Meeting times for this semester are posted on w3vc.org!

name: Catholic Newman Club
description: The Catholic Newman Club is a social, religious, and service organization for students, staff, and faculty who are Catholic or otherwise interested in the faith.

name: Center for Leadership Studies
description: The Center for Leadership Studies at Carnegie Mellon University helps students grow as effective and ethical leaders by offering courses, workshops, coaching, and mentoring that build skills such as vision, communication, reflection, and decision-making.

name: Center for Shared Prosperity (CSP)
description: The Center for Shared Prosperity helps CMU and the Pittsburgh community work together on projects that lower barriers to equity and prosperity. With compassion, collaboration and curiosity we engage with our neighbors to achieve shared prosperity in our region.

name: Central Church of Pittsburgh Korean Ministry
description: The Central Church of Pittsburgh Korean Ministry aims to promote cultural awareness and appreciation among students by organizing a Christian Ministry. This mission seeks to foster unity, celebrate diversity, and provide an opportunity for students to learn about the Bible and foster spiritual growth. Here are the key activities and goals of this mission: 1. Korean Food and Prayer: begin the event with a prayer or meditation session led by the club members, emphasizing gratitude and unity.2. Prayer Requests and Blessings: provide an opportunity for students to share their prayer requests or intentions and Offer blessings, prayers, or positive affirmations for those seeking spiritual support3. Fun activities: provide a space to hang out, make new friends, and appreciate the diversity of perspectives.

name: Central Church Student Fellowship
description: Central Church exists to make and mature disciples of Jesus Christ, so that the Gospel of Jesus Christ is the foundational principle through which we live in our homes, in our church, in our work, and in the city of Pittsburgh.

name: Chabad of CMU
description: The purpose of Chabad of CMU is to promote positive Jewish activities and views within the university community. Chabad of CMU is also a home away from home for Jews at Carnegie Mellon University. Through social, educational and spiritual programming, Chabad helps strengthen their Jewish awareness, pride, and identity, helping them establish a life-long, personal connection to Judaism.

name: Chess Club
description: Our mission is to unite the chess community at CMU and introduce new players to the game. We aim to foster a welcoming environment for chess enthusiasts of all levels and provide resources for players to improve their skills.

name: Chinese Bible Study Fellowship
description: To make a lasting impact for God’s kingdom by helping students, faculty, and people from across the world come to know Jesus Christ. Our meetings are conducted in Chinese, but all are welcome.

name: Chinese Student Association
description: Chinese Student Association's primary mission is to provide Chinese undergraduate students with a homely community and help them adjust to life here at Carnegie Mellon University. CSA also promotes Chinese culture and maintains open social platforms for all students. CSA's activities include but are not limited to providing incoming Chinese students with orientation information; hosting internal and external events within the CMU community; hosting events to celebrate traditional Chinese holidays & festivals; providing its members with professional help; and creating social opportunities to facilitate interactions between members and the campus community.

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association
description: The mission of CSSA is bring everyone together by holding external and internal events that create opportunities for Chinese students and scholars as well as students from other country to meet each other and establish mutual-beneficial relationships. Our organization aims to provide a second home for international students from China and familiarize Chinese students with CMU as well as the Pittsburgh community. We are dedicating at being supportive and inclusive for all students, from undergraduates, doctoral students to visiting scholars at Carnegie Mellon University and people in our community. We also hope to show the beauty of Chinese traditional culture to the rest of CMU community through our events. For new students, we help them to adapt to the new social and academic environment via a series of orientation events. For old students, we assist them to keep connected to the community and find new encounters by organizing a variety of social and cultural events. In general, by participating in our activities that facilitate cultural adaptation and help bridge the cultural gap, Chinese students will blend into the community better. In addition, members of CSSA will improve their communication skills, build up problem-solving abilities, obtain supportive peer groups, and make lifelong friends through involvement in event design, planning, and implementation.

name: Christian Business Association
description: The Christian Business Association (CBA) is meant to serve the Tepper community as the nexus where Christianity and Business interact.

name: Christians on Campus
description: The mission of the club is to facilitate gatherings of students who are interested in learning about, experiencing, and enjoying Jesus Christ and the Bible.

name: Circle K
description: Circle K International is committed to developing leaders by offering unique service, leadership, and fellowship opportunities that allow members to positively impact communities around the world and  help others feel like they have a place where they belong.

name: Climate Action Campaign at CMU
description: We believe in fighting to stop the current effects of climate change and advancing bold, effective solutions that create lasting impact. Guided by the UN’s 17 Sustainable Development Goals, we recognize that addressing the climate crisis cannot be achieved through individual actions alone; strong and effective climate policy is essential.

name: Club Baseball
description: Our mission is to field a competitive baseball team that works hard and plays their absolute best every time we take the field. We also want to make sure all of our players have fun while doing so as we are not only a team, but a family. We wish to respect the game through hard work and integrity while trying to be successful and an overall winning ball club.

name: Club Basketball
description: To offer students an outlet to competitively play basketball and compete against other university programs in addition to promoting the ideals of sportsmanship and teamwork.

name: CMQ+
description: CMQ+ (formerly Allies Grad) serves LGBTQIA+ graduate students at Carnegie Mellon University, though staff and/or allies are always welcome at our events. The + denotes allies, non-identifying, HIV+ and other s–open to interpretation and an avowal of both our inclusivity and focus on queer identifying people at CMU. We aim to help create an affirming and rewarding social and intellectual environment for this community, as well as tangible support for graduate students at Carnegie Mellon of all gender identities and sexual orientations. To accomplish these goals, CMQ+ works alone and with affiliated university groups to:A.	Act as an advocate to the University community for support of and issues relevant to individuals of all gender identities and sexual orientations. Work to overcome oppression and insensitivity on the basis of gender identity and sexual orientation.B.	Provide access for members of the University community to "safe places and safe people": individuals and forums for confidential discussion, support, and information.C.	Promote educational and professional development opportunities for LGBTQIA+ students, staff, and faculty, as well as parents and alumni.D.	Help find funding opportunities for CMQ+ and its members to attend conferences, workshops, and events.E.	Develop a broad appreciation for diversity and inclusiveness, as articulated in the Statement of Assurance, through promoting understanding of all gender identities and sexual orientations. Increase the awareness of discrimination and harassment on the basis of gender identity and sexual orientation in all contexts, personal, professional, and academic.F.	Provide all students, faculty, and staff with an avenue to demonstrate their support of individuals of all gender identities and sexual orientations.G.	Develop a professional network of alumni and current students in advancing these goals at CMU.

name: CMU Abhinaya
description: Our members are trained in Kuchipudi, Bharatanatyam, Odissi and Mohiniattam, among other styles of dance. We are united by a collective passion to convey stories, experiences and emotions through movement. CMU Abhinaya is a space to connect with one's Indian heritage, build community and practice physical and mental well-being. We perform at several events during the school year including Diwali, Holi and Dancer's Symposium. Our vision for this year is to expand our presence on campus and be a part of bigger communities and events.

name: CMU Bhangra
description: The purpose of Carnegie Mellon University Bhangra (CMU Bhangra) is to provide an opportunity and instruction for aspiring dancers, and to represent CMU in a positive light in an international community through cultural expression.

name: CMU Blackjack Society
description: To provide a venue for people to practice Blackjack strategy and related card counting skills.

name: CMU Blockchain Group
description: Carnegie Mellon Blockchain is focused on developing and understanding the decentralized future and being the hub of all crypto and blockchain activity on Carnegie Mellon's campus.

name: CMU Board Game Club
description: CMU Board Game Club -- We play games.  We provide a venue for the practice of board-, card-, and other forms of social tabletop games. We meet every Saturday in the Foster Dining room on the 2nd floor of the UC, directly above Entropy (backup Zoom link: https://cmu.zoom.us/j/93450695745?pwd=L25TUHhVK2RrSFlDbWh5T2NScUplQT09). We begin at 1:00 pm. Gaming lasts until everyone goes home. To join our mailing list, sign up here: https://lists.andrew.cmu.edu/mailman/listinfo/board-game-club

name: CMU Business Technology Group
description: Business Technology Group is an organization at Carnegie Mellon University that focuses on providing undergraduates and graduate students that are interested in business and technology with real-world projects, a strong understanding of potential career paths, and offer them opportunities to gain hands-on experience in their areas of interest. BTG specializes in applied technology that improves work, play, and education in Pittsburgh.

name: CMU ChemE Car
description: The students of the ChemE Car, a student project team, maintain a goal of designing, building, and testing a small-scale automobile that is powered and controlled by chemical means to compete in regional and national competitions sponsored by AIChE. All majors are welcome!

name: CMU ChemE Cube
description: The ChemE Cube team aims to design, build, and test a 1 cubic foot plant that produces a desired product based on an annual problem statement provided by the RAPID Manufacturing Institute. The team competes at the annual competition held during the AIChE Student Conference through a head-to-head duel and creation of an ad, poster, and pitch presented to judges during the competition.

name: CMU Climbing Club
description: The CMU Climbing Club’s mission is to foster a community of casual and competitive climbers. Making extensive use of indoor facilities, and venturing outdoors responsibly we hope to ensure that any student regardless of their identity, background or experience can have fun and pursue a fulfilling climbing career.

name: CMU Cubing Club
description: The organization aims to create a welcoming environment focused on promoting twisty puzzles, Rubik’s cubes, and speedcubing activities.

name: CMU Cyphers
description: The main purpose of the CMU Cypher Club (hitherto referred to as CMU Cyphers) is to provide a comfortable, low-commitment environment for people to share their talents at rapping and hear the work of others. It will also give people an opportunity to give and receive feedback, as well as practice, to improve their own abilities. This club is open to all members of the university community.  All activities and functions of the organization must be legal under University, local, state, and federal laws.

name: CMU Data Structures and Algorithms Club
description: Our mission is to foster a collaborative environment where CMU students can deepen their understanding of data structures and algorithms, raise interest in solving interesting algorithmic problems, develop robust problem-solving skills, and prepare for academic and professional success in computer science and related fields.

name: CMU Debate
description: CMU debate aims to foster a community of individuals interested in speech and rhetoric and promote the value of public speaking to the greater CMU community.

name: CMU Esports
description: CMU Esports is a student organization at Carnegie Mellon University that strives to offer both a competitive and casual gaming scene that is accessible to all students. We recruit and train highly competitive collegiate teams for all popular games while hosting gaming events of all shapes and sizes on campus for all students.

name: CMU Europe Club
description: Whether it is the learning of a European language, cultural education, or tips for an upcoming exchange to a European country – the Europe Club's mission is to connect people, help networking, and broaden an international network, as well as educate about European culture, politics, history, or language.

name: CMU Explorers Club
description: We are a student run organization dedicated to bringing together people with an interest in the outdoors since 1959. We venture into the wilderness through organized activities such as rock and ice climbing, mountaineering, caving, hiking, backpacking, flat and white water boating, sailing, skydiving, mountain biking, scuba diving, and more.

name: cmu filmmakers
description: At CMU Filmmakers, we bring together aspiring storytellers to learn the art of filmmaking through hands-on experience. Our mission is to offer fun workshops where students can learn about Movie/TV pitching, writing and production from industry professionals.

name: CMU Fishing Club
description: The CMU fishing club's mission is to promote the sport and prosperity of fishing at CMU. This is through educational sessions with older members on proper and sustainable fishing practices and fishing outings. The CMU fishing club aims to make it easy and convenient for students to partake in humane and ethical fishing.

name: CMU Futsal Club
description: Our mission is to offer both competitive and recreational opportunities to play futsal and foster a fun competitive culture.

name: CMU Global
description: To promote, support and create opportunities and student led initiatives for students to connect and network with their peers on all CMU campuses across the globe.

name: CMU Green Practices
description: We work to advance operational sustainability efforts across all aspect of university support services and offer programming to foster a campus culture of environmental sustainability.

name: CMU Hindu YUVA
description: The mission of the CMU Hindu YUVA organization is to bring together both Hindu and non-Hindu students to raise awareness about what Hinduism is. As one of the largest and oldest religions in the world, we believe it is necessary that there be an organization on campus to represent the interests of the Hindu community present here.

name: CMU HOX Business Fraternity
description: Eta Omega Chi(HOX) aims to provide professional developments and connections to its members and build a tunnel for mutually beneficial relationships between the graduated alumni and current students for professional purposes.

name: CMU Italian Society
description: The CMU Italian Society aims to promote Italian culture, foster a community for Italian and Italian-interested students, and provide academic, professional, and social opportunities through various events and initiatives.

name: CMU Jiya
description: CMU Jiya is Carnegie Mellon's premier award-winning all-girls competitive Bollywood fusion team, combining a variety of different dance styles from western styles like hip-hop and contemporary, to Indian classical and fold dances. Our dancers aim to exhibit our passion for dance and showcase their love for Indian culture. The troupe's members excel at a variety of dance styles and many have been dancing since childhood, but some have just begun dancing as recently as when they joined Jiya. The team participates in many on-campus and off-campus performances throughout the year, as well as competitions in the national collegiate circuit. We placed 2nd at Steel City Sapna with our 2020-2021 set!

name: CMU KSEA
description: Korean-American Scientists and Engineers Association (KSEA) was established in 1971 as a non-profit professional organization and has grown to over 6,000 registered members with ~ 70 Local Chapter and Branches, ~30 Affiliated Professional Societies, and 13 Technical Groups across the United States. The organization's objectives are:- Promoting the application of science and technology for the general welfare of society- Foster international cooperation especially between the U.S. and Korea- Help Korean-American Scientists and Engineers develop their full career potentialKSEA has set up multiple satellite campus groups across the nation, namely universities with a sizeable Korean-American student population. The establishment of KSEA at CMU will allow for a national organization to back those at CMU who identify with these goals and create this safe space. The group will meet to push forward these objectives and encourage collaboration and progression for Korean-American Scientists and Engineers.

name: CMU Marathon Club
description: Our mission is to build a community of long-distance road runners who support each other on our marathon journeys. We will engage with our runners and national community to provide our members the best training and race opportunities possible.

name: CMU Mumon Karate
description: One of the oldest martial arts clubs on the East Coast, teaching traditional Shotokan-style Karate. Founded in the mid-1960s, the club has a rich lineage tracing back to Master Gichin Funakoshi and Waseda University under the long-term leadership of Master Sadaharu Honda. Today, the student organization practices twice a week on campus, offering members a community to learn self-defense, improve physical fitness, and pursue the mental discipline of Budo ('the way').

name: CMU OM - An Indian Cultural Organization
description: OM is an independent non-sectarian forum for the education and promotionof the Indian cultural and spiritual heritage. Students, faculty, and all other members ofthe Carnegie Mellon community are welcome to join. OM provides cultural and spiritualassistance to Hindus, Jains, Sikhs, and other cultures, backgrounds and religions of Indian origin.OM also provides opportunities for non-academic individuals to work and identify closely withthe objectives and programs of Indian cultures and religions such as Hinduism, Jainism, andSikhism. We host events, trips, and conversations open to all individuals on campus.

name: CMU Photography Club
description: Photography Club aims to give photographers on campus a community such that they can share their art and work with other photographers on campus. The reason we want this club to be created is due to the fact that there seems to be no organization that is aimed towards photographers on campus. There is evidence of a former photography club on campus years ago but seems to have dissipated and no longer an established or recognized organization. This club aims to bring events like photowalks and photo exhibitions. This would then promote the works of the many talented photographers we have on campus and allow them to foster their creativity.

name: CMU Pickleball
description: The mission of this club is to connect people who play or are interested in playing pickleball at CMU as well as introduce the game to new players. Our activities include but are not limited to: pickleball lessons, organized singles and doubles match play, and tournaments. This club will also provide equipment for players who need it.

name: CMU Pool Club
description: Our mission is to promote the sport of pool at the collegiate level and bolster the existing community of pool enthusiasts. We are also resurging our pool team such that players' interest in competing at a high level becomes feasible.

name: CMU Powerlifting
description: The goal of this club is to provide both avid and new lifters a safe community to grow stronger together at CMU. Through group practices, meets, and clinics, CMU Powerlifting promises to foster an uplifting and supportive atmosphere for all members to become the best lifters they can be.All activities and functions of the organization must be legal under University, local, state, and federal laws.

name: CMU Private Equity & Venture Capital Club
description: The Club aims to strengthen the private equity and venture capital ecosystem at Carnegie Mellon University by promoting collaboration, education, and professional development for students and alumni. We connect private market firms with students, offer educational resources, and provide access to industry experts. Furthermore, we encourage interdisciplinary engagement across various schools to build a vibrant network of private investment enthusiasts.

name: CMU Raasta
description: CMU Raasta is a dance team committed to promoting Indian culture across campus. Through the West Indian folk dance garba-raas, we aim to spread awareness of our traditions. By performing on and off campus at dance competitions throughout the nation, we wish to exhibit the talent and diversity of Carnegie Mellon students.

name: CMU Recreational Badminton Club
description: The CMU Recreational Badminton Club strives to offer a free and comfortable environmentfor players of all levels to engage in badminton recreationally.In addition, the club aims to advocate for badminton andrecreational badminton players in the CMU community.The club also will promote an awareness of the sport andfoster respect for all other athletes.

name: CMU Red Cross
description: The American Red Cross prevents and alleviates human suffering in the face of emergencies by mobilizing the power of volunteers and the generosity of donors.

name: CMU RPG Association
description: The CMU RPG Association aims to support fans of role-playing games on the Carnegie Mellon University campus.

name: CMU Saans
description: CMU Saans is Carnegie Mellon's premier South Asian a cappella team, coming together to share our passion for music and celebrate South Asian culture. Performing both at on campus showcases as well as in competitions across the country, we hope to continue representing Carnegie Mellon on a national level. We also aim to spread awareness about South Asian culture through the medium of music, while also finding a platform to express our cultural identity.

name: CMU Sailing Club
description: We are dedicated to bringing together people with an interest in sailing and to teach new members the fundamentals.

name: CMU Science Olympiad
description: The purpose of Science Olympiad at Carnegie Mellon University is to inspire interest in the studyof science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) among youth by organizing anddirecting Science Olympiad tournaments on campus.

name: CMU Shopno: Bengali Student Association
description: CMU Shopno is an organization that is dedicated to honoring and educating the CMU community on Bengali culture.

name: CMU Skateclub
description: Skate club creates a safe space for skaters of all ages and skill levels/abilities to skate together, hangout, and express themselves through the sport. We help promote and legitimize skating as a sport as well as have it taken more seriously as a form of exercise. We host fundraising events, lessons, and other skating related activities to connect members.

name: CMU Special Olympics
description: The Special Olympics strives to create a better world by fostering the acceptance and inclusion of all people: through the power of sports, people with intellectual disabilities discover new strengths and abilities, skills, and success. The Three Rivers Region branch of Special Olympics has hosted its regional competition, the Western Spring Sectional, on the Carnegie Mellon University campus since 2016. This student-run organization aims to bolster student, faculty, and CMU engagement to volunteer for the Western Spring Sectional. We assist Special Olympics' Three Rivers Region in recruiting volunteers and committee leads for the event, lead on-campus marketing strategies to the general CMU population, and engage with CMU clubs and faculty to promote the Western Spring Sectional event. Our organization is advised by the Regional Sports Directors of the Special Olympics Three Rivers Region.

name: CMU Taekwondo Club
description: The Carnegie Mellon University Sport Taekwondo Club was formed on behalf of the Carnegie Mellon University community. It is an organization that pursues the tenets of the World Taekwondo Federation (WTF), the international governing body of Olympic-style taekwondo. Thus, we feel that the absence of a World Taekwondo Federation taekwondo club deprives the campus community of complete taekwondo-presence, and we therefore commit ourselves to the exposition of this sport to Carnegie Mellon University.

name: CMU Star Wars
description: To build a community of Star Wars fans across the CMU galaxy.

name: CMU Street Styles
description: CMU Street Styles, est. 2003 as the International Freestylers, is Carnegie Mellon University’s student organization dedicated to teaching and engaging in street dance and hip hop culture. Street Styles strives to bring the knowledge and love of street dance and hip hop culture to as many members of the CMU and broader Pittsburgh dance community as possible. Members of this organization will regularly practice their chosen dance styles, perform for their peers to showcase their progress, and support one another in- and outside of dance.

name: CMU Summit on US-China Innovation and Entrepreneurship
description: As a non-profit student organization, our mission is to connect innovators and entrepreneurs world-wide via events including CMU Summit, Demo Day, Global Opportunities Fair, Hack-a-Startup, and more.Our annual summit, this year held between April 23-25, is a high-profile forum that gathers leading entrepreneurs, venture capitalists, and scholars from the US and China to address real-world challenges and discussing opportunities in various technology and business fields, in addition to providing start-ups with communication and recruitment channels.We encourage communications and collaborations between the US and China, support talents from both countries, and facilitate business and technological developments.

name: CMU Surf Club
description: CMU Surf Club hopes to create a community where surfers and those with a love for the water can come together to share their interests through weekend trips and events. We hope that this space will help people with these interests find another source of enjoyment to take their minds of our their professional and academic affairs.

name: CMU Table Tennis Club
description: Our mission is to grow table tennis at the collegiate level and bolster the existing local table tennis communities. We also strive to be an open gateway to new players while housing a team that can compete at the highest possible level.

name: CMU Tennis Club
description: Our mission is to foster a community for tennis on campus for players of all levels and also provide a more competitive environment for intermediate to advanced level players.

name: CMU Transportation Club
description: The Carnegie Mellon University Transportation Club aims to expose students to transportation-related classes; promote transportation research opportunities; to be a platform for networking and collaboration amongst students, faculty and industry leaders; and to raise awareness of employment opportunities in the transportation industry.

name: CMU Vegan Society
description: Connecting Through Food, Activism, and Social Events

name: CMU Wushu Club
description: The mission of CMU Wushu is to become a supportive place for anyone who is interested in either learning about or practicing wushu. We focus on taolu, which is a performative, non-contact sport.

name: cmuTV
description: cmuTV provides clubs on campus with livestream and recording capabilities! We also maintain a Youtube account with hundreds of videos dating back more than a decade, working with Dancer's Symposium, Lunar Gala, Buggy, and more. To its members cmuTV provides a creative outlet, an opportunity to learn new skills, and a friendly, curious community. To the campus community at large, cmuTV provides essential services including professional video recording and equipment rentals.

name: College Democrats
description: The Carnegie Mellon University chapter of the College Democrats organization dedicated to electing Democrats across the country using the power of the student voice.

name: ColorStack at CMU
description: ColorStack’s mission is to increase the entrance, retention, and successof Black, Latinx, and Native American students in computing throughcommunity building, academic support, and professional development.

name: Community & Inclusion
description: Provide the community with information regarding programs and events related to equity, diversity, inclusion and belonging.

name: Computational Biology Society (CBS)
description: The Carnegie Mellon Computational Biology Undergraduate Society (CMU-CBUS) is committed to bringing computational biology majors and students interested in computational biology together in a welcoming and active community through academic and social events. CMU-CBUS will organize events to showcase computational biology research at CMU and beyond, plan socials to foster connections between computational biology students and faculty, and support students in attending computational biology conferences (e.g. ISMB). CMU-CBUS will maintain a close relationship with Carnegie Mellon University's Ray and Stephanie Lane Computational Biology Department, and serve as a liaison between CB students and department.All activities and functions of the organization must be permitted by University Policy and legal under local, state, and federal laws.

name: Computer Club
description: CMU Computer Club is for those who get hyped about finding nifty programming projects to hack on, contributing to free and open source software, and tinkering with vintage machines like Amigas, VAXen, and SPARCs. They run a student-operated datacenter alongside groups like KGB, ScottyLabs, and PPP, and hold Open Hacking Hours every Saturday around 17:00 in Cyert B7. Join their mailing list at members-subscribe@club.cc.cmu.edu or visit cmucc.org to stay in the loop on events and classic hacker film screenings.

name: Computer Graphics & Animation Club
description: The Computer Graphics and Animation (CGA) club is a club for analogue and digital enthusiasts interested in exploring motion-based and physics-based graphics, such as those seen in Disney/Pixar movies and video games today. We hope to foster a learning community for those interested in traditional animation, as well as various 2D and 3D software programs such as Blender and Maya.

name: Consortium for Graduate Student in Management
description: In collaboration with the Consortium since 2001, the Tepper School of Business empowers aspiring leaders from underrepresented ethnic groups by providing a transformative business education and fostering a network of success. We commit to increasing diversity within the business landscape and developing future generations of ethical and impactful leaders.

name: Consulting Club
description: Our mission is to educate, connect and empower students and prepare them for success in both consulting and internal strategy careers. We are dedicated to providing interactive, customized and inclusive programming that:- Introduces members to the industry and potential career paths- Empowers students to find their brand and share their stories- Supports and enables students of all cultural and professional backgrounds- Provides guidance and resources to prepare students for recruiting

name: Cosplay@CMU
description: We want to inspire makers, crafters, and artists in the CMU community to engage in the art of cosplay. Through workshops, weekly meetings, and community events, we hope to introduce cosplay to the greater CMU community.

name: Counterpoint
description: Counterpoint was founded in 2000 by Carolyn Weger as Carnegie Mellon's first all treble voices identifying a cappella group. In 2008, we founded the AcaPittsburgh benefit concert, and we have been holding concerts to benefit local causes and non-profits every year since. We also compete at the International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella (ICCA) annually. In 2023, Counterpoint placed second at the ICCA Central Quarterfinals and moved onto Semifinals in Buffulo, NY.

name: Cru
description: Cru is a community where the gospel captures hearts, transforms lives, and launches men and women into a lifelong adventure with Jesus Christ.

name: Culinary Club
description: We are a club of foodies, cooking aficionados, and culinary enthusiasts. We bring people together over the enjoyment of delicious meals, information exchange, and network around a common interest – food. We are the Culinary Club, and we are dedicated to enabling you to make the most of your Pittsburgh food experience!

name: Dancers' Symposium
description: Dancers' Symposium (DS) aims to provide an open and supportive environment to further the exposure of dance, music, student choreography, and student performance at Carnegie Mellon University. DS is CMU's largest student-run dance organization, with over 180 members each semester.

name: DART (Defense, Aerospace, Robotics & Tech Club)
description: Connecting CMU students with defense, aerospace, robotics, and dual-use tech through HQ visits, guest speakers, projects, and treks.DART (Defense, Aerospace, Robotics, Tech) is a student club that helps CMU students explore careers and technologies in defense, aerospace, robotics, and dual-use industries. We connect students with local companies through HQ visits and guest speakers, and we run treks, workshops, and case competitions. Everyone is welcome to join. No industry knowledge is necessary.

name: Data Analytics Club
description: The mission of the Data Analytics Club is to facilitate and grow students' appreciation for, knowledge of, and access to Data Analytics resources and opportunities (and all relevant applications therein), and empower members with managerial-level analytical skills that will make them effective in any role or industry.

name: Department of Athletics
description: The Carnegie Mellon University Department of Athletics, Physical Education, and Recreation will provide a transformative and inclusive educational experience that nurtures competitiveness, wellness, belonging and resilience, and provides students with a leadership framework to live healthy and impactful lives.

name: Design For America
description: Design For America x CMU is a group of interdisciplinary students who use their unique design, engineering, and social sciences perspectives to confront social issues in innovative ways.As a chapter of national DFA, we use the human centered design process empathize and work with our users we research, ideate, prototype, test and develop solutions.We collaborate in teams of students with diverse backgrounds and interests to develop well-rounded solutions and fuse collaboration, leadership, and creativity.Student-led teams partner closely with local community organizations identify challenging social issues to design and develop comprehensive solutions that benefit the broader community.

name: Doctors of Carnegie Society
description: The purpose of the Doctors of Carnegie Society is to provide students with information related to the health professions and professional school application process, to increase health awareness, and to be of beneficial service to the Carnegie Mellon University campus community.

name: Economics Club
description: To promote interest in the field of economics and its analytical mode of thought, to engage students' thinking and discussion skills through club dialogue and talks with professors and professionals, to introduce undergraduates to the various branches of the discipline and opportunities on and off campus.

name: Emergency Medical Service
description: The primary purpose of CMU EMS is to provide professional, high quality, timely, reliable andappropriate pre-hospital treatment for medical emergencies within our coverage area. The secondary purpose of the organization is to provide a community for EMS providers at Carnegie Mellon as well as an opportunity for those providers to practice emergency medicine. The tertiary purposes of the organization include training members of CMU EMS, the Carnegie Mellon community, and surrounding communities in emergency medical skills, coordinating training with other medical organizations, and providing community outreach training in injury awareness and prevention to reduce the overall incidence of injury and illness that requires emergency medical attention.

name: Emerging Leaders
description: A long-standing CMU tradition for first-year students, Emerging Leaders (EL) promotes the development of leadership skills through the lens of community engagement and social action.

name: Energy & CleanTech Club
description: Our mission is to (1) connect MBA students, industry professionals and Carnegie Mellon's energy innovation programs to foster interdisciplinary collaboration and (2) provide our members with the resources to learn, network and succeed in careers in the rapidly-evolving energy and cleantech landscapes

name: Engineering Ambassadors Network - Carnegie Mellon University
description: Our mission is to inspire future generations of engineers here in Pittsburgh. Our engineering students at CMU deliver presentations on exciting STEM topics in classrooms and on campus with engaging hands-on activities.

name: Engineers Without Borders
description: The Carnegie Mellon Chapter of EWB-USA seeks to better our members, our university, and our world by implementing socially and environmentally sustainable engineering projects in partnership with our community and developing communities around the world.

name: Environment & Water Resources Institute (EWRI) Graduate Student Chapter at CMU
description: The EWRI CMU Graduate Chapter allows students and professionals to connect and share information on planning, designing, and constructing environmentally sustainable infrastructure impacting water resources. This chapter will have various events throughout the year both on campus and in Pittsburgh to develop professional and networking skills.

name: Ethiopian Eritrean Student Association
description: The Ethiopian and Eritrean Association (EESA) is committed to promoting cultural unity, celebrating diversity, and supporting fundraising efforts for Ethiopian and Eritrean communities abroad. EESA provides a home and space for Ethiopian and Eritrean students to come together. Additionally, EESA fosters a welcoming community that embraces and shares the vibrant traditions and heritage of Ethiopia and Eritrea among students and the broader campus.

name: Feminists Engaged in Multicultural Matters and Education
description: The purpose of CMU FEMME (Feminists Engaged in Multicultural Matters and Education) is to educate the campus community on societal marginalization using an intersectional feminist lens to provide focus. The organization accomplishes its mission by providing educational programming, events, discussions, and entertainment relating to various intersectional issues, as well as fostering a safe space for all community members.FEMME is also the proud founder and member of the CMU Gender Equity Coalition. This is a new coalition of CMU student organizations that are interested in and committed to feminism, gender equity, and intersectional advocacy.

name: Fencing Club
description: The CMU Fencing Club exists to promote the sport of fencing on the CMU campus.  We aim to provide a safe environment for fencers to develop their skills and participate in local and regional tournaments.  Fencers of all skills levels and backgrounds are welcomed and trained to compete in collegiate level events.

name: Filipino Student Association
description: The Filipino Student Association aims to promote and educate people about Filipino culture.
