We provide a space for students to connect and bond through food, arts & crafts, karaoke, and our overall appreciation of the Philippines. Whether you are from Manila or just want to explore the culture, we hope to serve as an inclusive community for everyone to discover and celebrate what Filipino culture has to offer.

name: Film Club
description: Film Club's mission is to bring cinema to the Carnegie Mellon community in order to create a space for open and constructive dialogue surrounding film and its topics, as well as increase a spirit of film appreciation in the student body. We aim to showcase underrepresented minorities in hopes of exposing audiences to and educating them about experiences and cultures that aren't often shown on the big screen. Foci include women directors, queer directors, cult films, documentaries, and international cinema. Screenings will be followed by moderated discussions.

name: FirstTogether
description: The mission of the FIRST Together program is to enhance the overall success of first generation college students on our campus. Through our intentional and proactive efforts, we offer student-centered opportunities designed to support the academic, professional and campus engagement experiences of our students.

name: Freedom By Design
description: "Freedom by Design”, the AIAS community service program in partnership with the National Council of Architectural Registration Boards (NCARB), uses the talents of architecture students to radically impact the lives of people in their community through modest design and construction solutions. The program embraces efforts to provide both design-build and engagement solutions to address 5 barriers:PhysicalEducationalEnvironmentalSocio-EconomicCulturalFreedom by Design├óÔÇ×┬ó (FBD) encourages students to serve their communities by addressing issues with design solutions. FBD provides real-world experience through working with clients, learning from local licensed architects and contractors, and experiencing the practical impacts of architecture and design."

name: Fringe
description: Fringe was founded in 1969 by a group of architecture students at Carnegie Mellon University. At the time, it was the first independent student organization to compete in the history of Spring Carnival at CMU. Since then, Fringe has evolved into an all-inclusive group of friends who participate competitively in a variety of campus traditions and co-curricular activities. We are heavily involved in buggy, booth and intramural sports, along with a host of other on-campus events. We  are active year-round and have membership from across all of the colleges at CMU.

name: Furries@CMU
description: Our mission is to support the interests of and provide a safe and welcoming social environment for the furry community within Carnegie Mellon University.

name: Futurist Club
description: CMU Futurist Club is a space for people of any academic background to gather and share their ideas for what they believe the future should be like. Together we learn frameworks and techniques to practice foresight and speculation and have open minded discussions to better define what your preferred future looks like. Individually, you apply your learnings to your discipline to take steps toward that future.

name: Game Creation Society
description: Game Creation Society (GCS) is a student-run organization at Carnegie Mellon University, dedicated to the development of games. We exist to help members of all skill levels and backgrounds interested in making games learn more about and gain experience in game development. We├óÔé¼Ôäóre fostering a community around the development of student games and provide opportunities to learn, collaborate, get employed, and have fun.

name: Gender Minorities and Women in Physics
Girls Who Code
description: GWC CMU is an educational outreach non-profit, working in collaboration with the international organization Girls Who Code. GWC CMU is dedicated to closing the gender gap in technology by creating an environment where women in STEM feel empowered and enabling young women with the skill sets to challenge boundaries.

name: Global Business Brigades
description: Global Business Brigades provides business consulting and strategic counseling to support under-resourced micro-enterprises in developing countries. Not only do we address issues such as accounting, operations, and management skills but also we work side-by-side with community members to afford volunteers the opportunity for cultural exchange, micro-finance, and consulting experience.GBB has a tremendous impact on CMU students. The experience of traveling to another country with a great group of friends, getting to know countless hardworking individuals in a country that likely speaks another language, and using our unique skills to help their community as much as we can is an unforgettable memory for students. Not only are we continuing the CMU mission of using our education to do good, but also we are strengthening CMU's reputation abroad and creating a lasting impression within ourselves to make a positive impact in the world.

name: Google Developer Student Club
description: Google Developer Student Clubs are community groups for college and university students interested in Google developer technologies.Students from all undergraduate or graduate programs with an interest in growing as a developer are welcome. By joining a GDSC, students grow their knowledge in a peer-to-peer learning environment and build solutions for local businesses and their community.Google Developer Student Clubs is a program supported by Google Developers.

name: Graduate Biomedical Engineering Society
description: Building Community within graduate Students of the Biomedical Engineering Department through collaborative projects with student groups in and around the BME Department.

name: Graduate Business Association
description: Mission Statement And BylawsWe, the MBA students of the Carnegie Mellon University Tepper School of Business, establish this Charter and By-Laws for the Carnegie Mellon University Tepper School of Business Graduate Business Association (GBA) Student Government.The GBA is hereby established as a means of representing the interests of the student body of the Carnegie Mellon University business masters programs. Through the establishment of an elected Student Government, it shall provide a platform for the discussion of issues of student concern and act as the instrument by which student interests may be voiced to the administration and faculty of the MBA program and Carnegie Mellon University.The Student Government shall also act as an umbrella organization governing GBA supported student organizations and coordinating the distribution of funds distributed by the Tepper Administration to the student-run clubs and organizations recognized by the GBA.The Student Government represents the interests of all members of the GBA rather than any particular organization therein.

name: TepperCARES
description: TepperCares leads community service initiatives in the Greater Pittsburgh Area.  Our goal is to provide volunteers with high-quality service experiences that complement the MBA education.

name: Graduate Finance Association
description: We develop future leaders in finance by providing comprehensive mentoring, networking, and educational opportunities to our members. We have an engaged alumni network that extends across a breadth of firms and industries including, well respected investment banks, asset management firms, and Fortune 50 companies around the globe. Our goal is to promote a finance community at Tepper by hosting interactive events in order to provide a holistic perspective of the finance world.

name: Graduate Student Assembly
description: The Graduate Student Assembly (GSA) is the branch of student government that represents all graduate students at Carnegie Mellon University. The GSA’s mission is to advocate for and support the diverse needs of all CMU graduate students in their personal, professional, and public lives.

name: Grappling Club
description: The Grappling Club aims to provide students the opportunity to learn and practice grappling-based martial arts, specifically Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu, Judo, and Wrestling, in a safe setting. Regardless of experience or age, Grappling Club wants to help students to gain and hone their skills for self-defense or for competition, while developing self-discipline and improved mental and physical well-being.

name: Greek Sing
description: Greek Sing aims to stimulate philanthropic & community service efforts for the betterment of the Greek community, Carnegie Mellon campus community, and the Pittsburgh Community. For the 2024 and 2025 years, our partnered charity is Camp Kesem.

name: Habitat for Humanity
description: Habitat for Humanity at Carnegie Mellon seeks to empower volunteer-minded students to build homes, communities, and reason for hope. We strive for a world where everyone has a decent and dignified place to live.

name: Healthcare Club
description: The Tepper School of Business Healthcare Club seeks to create an engaged community of students interested in the biotechnology, pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and related healthcare fields. The club aims to connect students with companies and experts, facilitate networking opportunities, and provide targeted professional development opportunities.

name: Helix Dance Crew
description: The goals of this organization are to cultivate an increased interest in dance at CMU, host open classes that give members of the community the opportunity to dance, and push its dancers to develop their dance ability and give the best performance they can.

name: Hellenic Student Association
description: The Hellenic Student Association (HSA) at Carnegie Mellon aims to foster a community to learn about and to celebrate Greek culture through the exploration of traditions, history, language, and current events. HSA aspires to promote thought and connection amongst members. Our organization shares and exchanges music, food, and dance to further honor and appreciate Greek culture.

name: Here For You
description: Here for You aims to address issues regarding mental health on campus, primarily by encouraging healthy discussions about mental health, creating a peer support network, and connecting students to available resources. Our organization aims to have three focus areas. First, we will support those who are currently facing mental health related issues and empower them to seek professional help and other resources that both CMU and the Pittsburgh community offers. Second, we will advocate for those struggling with mental health issues by working to alleviate the stigma associated with mental illnesses and by becoming a liaison that works to bridge the gap that exists between students and the administration on mental health related issues. Third, we will educate the general student body to better understand what mental health disorders are thereby increasing awareness, teach how to recognize symptoms in a peer or oneself, and what resources are available on campus and in Pittsburgh.

name: Hillel Jewish Students Association
description: To enrich the lives of Jewish students and the CMU community so that they may enrich the Jewish people and the world.

name: Historical European Martial Arts Club
description: Our mission is to bring the sport of HEMA fencing to the CMU community.  We aim to teach people the art of fencing in the historical styles of medieval Europe.  Compared to arts such as olympic fencing which focus primarily on the sports side of things, we also have a historical aspect to our art which spans centuries and can help to put into perspective why the weapons we practice with developed in the ways that they did.  We have frequent group discussions about why certain techniques which were so popular in the manuscripts we reference don't work as well nowadays in sport fencing.  These discussions allow members to better their understanding of the rationale behind techniques, and become better fencers and athletes as a result.

name: History Graduate Student Association
description: The mission of the History Graduate Student Organization is to represent history graduate students on campus, to advocate for their needs, and to support networking with history graduate students at other institutions.

name: Hong Kong Student Association
description: The Hong Kong Student Association at Carnegie Mellon University is an organisation for all those who are interested in  Hong Kong culture and its traditions. HKSA is devoted to fostering a stronger Asian community and bringing Hong Kong awareness to campus. As a recognised group in CMU, HKSA strives to bring you fun, meaningful, cultural, and social events throughout the year; while providing opportunities for people who are interested or from Hong Kong to build meaningful relationships.

name: Humanist League
description: The Carnegie Mellon University Humanist League is dedicated to building a community of humanists, who strive to improve the human condition through reason and compassion. We promote discussion over dogma through regular philosophical discussion meetings, and larger speaker events.

name: Ice Hockey
description: Unite players across the university to represent CMU and play hockey in a competitive league.

name: IDEA @ CMU (Inquiry, Discussion, Exploration, and Argument)
IEEE CMU Chapter
description: Our mission is to provide a social, technical, and professional community to the ECE student body. We want to help our ECE community gain knowledge, skills and networks that will help them through their academic and professional careers. While CMU IEEE holds many different kinds of events throughout the year, this year we are focusing mostly on social events.

name: Independent Musicians Organization
description: IMO is one of CMU’s most active student-run music orgs, helping students connect with other musicians, form bands with their friends, promote their work, play concerts and plug into the campus and greater Pittsburgh music scene. We host weekly Saturday jam sessions and organize monthly live music both on and off campus, with a mission to unite people through community-oriented programming and live events.

name: Indian Graduate Student Association
description: 1. Enhance cultural connection among Indian Students2. Help the Indian Cohort with any issues they come up with3. Organize festive celebrations to spread cultural and social awareness

name: Indonesian Students at CMU
description: To make ISCMU a warm, fun, and enlightening community for Indonesian students at CMU, as well as become a responsive and reliable channel about CMU and Indonesia for external stakeholders in Indonesia, United States, and international communities.

name: Industrial Design Society of America
description: Industrial Designers Society of America (IDSA) is the most prominent industrial design association in the US. The organization’s roots stretch to the beginning of the profession and its members are some of the most celebrated industrial designers of all time. The IDSA community helps strengthen the industrial design profession as a whole and contributes to the boundless impact of design within business, culture, and society.

name: INFORMS Student Chapter
description: CMU INFORMS will lead in the developments, dissemination and implementation of knowledge, basic and applied research and technologies in operations research, operations management, business analytics, the management sciences, and related methods of improving operational processes, decision-making, and management. INFORMS will strive to:i.	Support efforts to extend, unify and integrate related branches of knowledge and practiceii.	Support the free interchange of information relevant to purpose of the groupiii.	Promote greater use of this knowledge by all organizations public and privateiv.	Encourage the education of students and continuing education of practitioners in the field of knowledgev.	Promote high professional standards and integrity in all work that we do

name: Infra Dance Company
description: Hi Infriends!We're Infra, CMU's only intermediate/advanced contemporary dance group. While contemporary is our main focus, our dance styles also include ballet, jazz, tap, and heels. If you love to dance, you should come join us! Whether you are an undergraduate, master's student, or PhD, all students are welcome to join. We're essentially a big family, and are always looking to have new dancers come join us.Our weekly meetings consist of rehearsals & open classes, culminating in an end-of-year showcase during April. Additionally, we perform in Dancers' Symposium each semester and Lunar Gala, CMU's annual fashion show.Auditions are typically held during the second week of September for Fall Semester and during the third week of January for Spring Semester.Whether it be at our performances, open classes, or auditions, we hope to see you soon!

name: Inter A Cappella Council
description: Inter A Cappella Council represents the diverse, vibrant a cappella and music community at Carnegie Mellon University. Comprised of members from all 8 of CMU's student-run singing clubs, our goal is to promote and facilitate a cappella music at CMU by providing a platform for individual a cappella organizations to coordinate.

name: Inter-Fellowship Association
description: The purpose of Inter-Fellowship Association (IFA) is to unite Christians (including Christian groups) on campus by organizing campus-wide, inter-fellowship activities, interaction, and service, for the glory of God. We strongly believe that the unity of the Body of Christ at CMU is biblically necessary and our goal is to foster inter-fellowship relations between the many different Christian fellowships and Christian organization. Another way we serve is by acting as a liaison between surrounding churches and other organization, and the CMU Christian community.

name: Interfraternity Council
description: The Interfraternity Council (IFC) exists to advance conditions conducive to the success of its member chapters and to promote the progress of the fraternity experience. With this aim, the IFC actively encourages interfraternalism and collaboration with other fraternity/sorority governing councils and student governance bodies. Maintaining a positive working relationship and partnership with the campus administration, alumni and inter/national headquarters of member chapters is critical toward elevating the role of the IFC.

name: InterVarsity Christian Fellowship
description: In response to God's love, grace, and truth, our mission is to know God and make Him known. In line with this, our vision is to grow in love with God through God's Word, with all of God's people, regardless of ethnicity or culture, for God's purposes in the world.

name: Intervarsity Ignite Black Christian Ministry
description: CMU Ignite is a national Christian InterVarsity organization committed to cultivating personal relationships with Jesus and intentionally embodying His image to inspire others to Christ. As a safe and welcoming space, particularly for the Black and Latine community at CMU, we invite all people, regardless of faith or religious backgrounds, to partake in our pursuit of being God’s hands, feet, and voice on the earth — making disciples of all nations in accordance with His Word.

name: Iranian Student Association
description: The “Iranian Student Associations” strives toPromote the understanding of the social, traditional, and historical aspects ofIranian culture,Provide a source of union and support for the Iranian community, andHelp foster friendship among the different cultural groups and the Pittsburghcommunity in general.

name: Japanese Mahjong Club
description: Our mission is to celebrate Japanese culture through Mahjong, fostering skill development, inclusivity, and friendship within the CMU community. We aim to create a fun, welcoming space for all skill levels, engage in cultural outreach, and promote competitive excellence while emphasizing the recreational aspect of the game.

name: Japanese Student Association
description: JSA strives to spread awareness of traditional and modern Japanese culture throughout the university and the greater Pittsburgh area. The organization also aims to connect its members to Japan and Japanese culture, through building partnerships with Japan-related entities. For more information, go to cmujsa.com

name: Jazz Club
description: Our mission is to create an environment and network for musicians to explore and develop their interests in the American art form of jazz. We also aim to share our music with the CMU community, providing live entertainment, a relaxing setting, stress relief, and a community of people to spend time with.

name: Jewish Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of the Jewish Graduate Student Association (JGSA) shallbe to foster a strong Jewish graduate community to serve the Jewishgraduate students and families of Carnegie Mellon University. It servesthis purpose by hosting events that are of interest to Jewish graduatestudents, such as social gatherings, celebrations of Jewish holidays, andnetworking events.

name: Joyful Noise
description: To share the truth and the joy of the gospel through music

name: Juntos
description: Juntos is a small, tightly knit service group that works with Bridges to Community to participate in a week-long service trip each year, historically to Nicaragua or Dominican Republic. We center around service and awareness in these countries along with Latinx communities in the local Pittsburgh area.

name: K-Pop Dance Club
description: Our mission is to bring students together through dance and the celebration of Korean music and culture. Throughout the semester, we hold regular dance classes and social events open to all students, regardless of previous dance experience. At the end of the semester, we hold a showcase for our subunits and projects that have been practicing their dances all semester. Our showcase is always free to attend!

name: KGB
description: The KGB is a social club for geeks, nerds, artists, scientists, and all-around silly people. We have meetings and events every week, and we don't bite! Mostly.What we do is... well... hard to explain. We're famous for putting on giant games of Capture the Flag with Stuff (CtFwS) every semester, organizing the Underground Tour, being thoroughly silly, and pulling the occasional harmless prank.We're the kind of friends you can count on when you need a vast retinue of people in top hats and monocles to follow you through a library. We're also always happy to help each other out when school gets rough and everything needs to be confusing in a totally different way.For more information, come to a meeting at 5:00 pm on Mondays at MM 103. Or, join our Discord server (cmukgb.org/discord), check out our website (cmukgb.org), or follow us on Instagram (@cmu_kgb). See you around!------Founded in 1988 as the Booth counterpart to the CIA (Carnegie Involvement Association).

name: Kiltie Band
description: The Kiltie Band is Carnegie Mellon's Marching Band! We have a marching, concert, and pep bands and are open to all experience levels (no auditions!). As one of CMU's oldest student organizations, we pride ourselves on our iconic wacky traditions and making great music to support the athletics department!

name: Korean American Students' Association (KASA)
description: The mission of KASA is to spread awareness of Korean culture and increase appreciation for Korean culture across all groups at Carnegie Mellon University.

name: Korean Graduate Student Association
description: Our organization promotes academic collaboration and social interactions among the Korean graduate students at CMU. We also help Korean CMU graduate students develop their careers by connecting them to alumni, companies, and universities in Korea. Lastly, we provide information to new incoming Korean graduate students about Pittsburgh and CMU to help them get started.

name: Korean Student Association
description: The mission of the Korean Students Association of Carnegie Mellon University shall be to promote  Korean culture through cultural, social, political, and community service events. Our activities would range from providing informal social functions to promoting our educational and cultural foundation, which are intrinsically non-political and non-discriminating in nature.

name: Latin American Business Club
description: The mission of the LABC is to promote the Latin American culture to the Tepper community by presenting its members with the regional Latin American events that Pittsburgh offers, providing access for both United States and International students interested in learning about professional and academic opportunities in Latin American countries, growing club members to network by communicating with Alumni and Latin American leaders in the U.S. and Latin America, seeking opportunities to develop community service and volunteer programs for members in Latin America or related to Latin America, promoting diversity recruiting, and having great events with the Carnegie Mellon University community to promote the festive Latin spirit. The LABC is a conduit to promote diversity recruiting. Additionally, the LABC collaborates with the Tepper School of Business Admissions Department to provide support for prospective, admitted and incoming students.

name: Latino/a Graduate Student Association
description: To facilitate communication and coordination among the Latin@ community at CMU in order to foster collaboration in academic, cultural, professional and social subjects

name: Lean On Me at CMU
description: The organization's purpose is to create a safe space for the Carnegie Mellon community through operating a confidential non-crisis peer-to-peer mental health text hotline manned by trained peers.

name: Lemon
description: Carnegie Mellon University is widely known for its STEM programs but has a lack of understanding and promotion surrounding the arts. The university has a long history of producing a connection to the arts, however that connection tends to go unnoticed. Through our publication, we hope to provide a formal lens into that world. Lemon magazine aims to showcase student artistic practices in an effort to build a community not only for the artistic population at CMU, but also to extend beyond to the broader CMU and Pittsburgh population.

name: Lunar Gala
description: Lunar Gala began in 1997 at Carnegie Mellon University to celebrate the Chinese New Year and has evolved into a highly anticipated fashion production inspired by the Zodiac Animal of the Lunar Calendar. The organization intends to act as a cross-disciplinary opportunity for individuals across the Carnegie Mellon campus to develop skills ranging from clothing design, branding, communications, public relations, show production, teamwork, and more.

name: Magic@CMU
description: Our mission is to create a friendly and welcoming environment for playing the card game Magic: The Gathering.Join our Discord: https://discord.gg/U3mdyq2qKU

name: Masters of Flying Objects
description: The mission of The Masters of Flying Objects (AKA The CMU Juggling Club) is to bring together jugglers and other circus-y folks of all skill ranges together to learn from each other and practice catching flying things. In doing so, we hope to spread the joy of juggling, diabolo, unicycling, balloon animal-ing, slacklining, Kendama, devil sticks, club passing, balancing-on-one-footing, and general circus-isness to the community in CMU, Pitt, and Pittsburgh.

name: Mayur - South Asian Student Association
description: Our mission is to bring together the South Asian Community at CMU by hosting small and large scale cultural events throughout the semester. We hold smaller events like general board meetings and philanthropy events as well as larger ones like Diwali and Mock Shaadi. We are also a philanthropy organization as some of our proceeds go to charity.

name: MechE Community Engagement Committee
description: Our mission is to create a transformative environment for all members of the MechE community (Students, Faculty and Staff), regardless of status, race, ability, gender, national origin, or other affiliations. We aim to embed this in all that we do; in classrooms and curriculum, in workshops and events, and in 1:1 and group interactions.

name: Media & Entertainment Club
description: The Media & Entertainment Club (M&EC) at the Tepper School of Business welcomes all Carnegie Mellon University students to appreciate the work and art unique to the media and entertainment industries. We are a professional club for students that takes a holistic approach by providing career discovery, media appreciation, and entertainment-focused event opportunities for students. We value diversity of background, interests, thought, career direction, and even sense of humor.The Media & Entertainment Club strives to provide Tepper MBAs and graduate students exposure, knowledge, and skills to successfully develop careers in entertainment. The club also acts as the best resource for companies looking for talents who are eager to innovate in this fast-paced industry.

name: Mellon FIT
description: The mission of Mellon Fit is to provide opportunities that promote health, fitness, and productivity to students and faculty in and around Mellon Institute while facilitating cross-disciplinary interaction and communication between neighboring departments.

name: Men's Club Volleyball
description: Carnegie Mellon Club Volleyball Team's mission is to give its members theopportunity to build teamwork and leadership skills through the game of volleyball. This organization strives to attain a high level of athletic success while maintaining the higheststandard of sportsmanship, integrity, and team unity.

name: Men's Ultimate Frisbee Club
description: We aim to create a cohesive, fun, and competitive community around the sport of ultimate at CMU.

name: Men's Water Polo Club
description: Carnegie Mellon Men's Club Water Polo focuses on our members developing to become stronger water polo players, the team bonding and becoming more tight-knit through social gatherings, and on creating a competitive team to have success in tournaments. We compete in the Mid-Atlantic division of the Collegiate Water Polo Association and participate in tournaments throughout the fall and spring semesters. We have performed very well in last year's tournaments.

name: Middle Eastern and North African Student Association
description: With open arms and an inclusive spirit, MENASA encompasses a wider spectrum of cultures and traditions, uniting students from various Middle Eastern and North African countries. Our club reflects the rich culture of our region, embracing those who share our heritage.In MENASA, diversity of tongues is celebrated. Whether you're fluent in Arabic, speak another MENA language, or don't speak any at all, you are warmly welcomed to join our vibrant community.Come and be a part of this exciting journey to learn more about the Middle Eastern and North African student community.

name: Mock Trial
description: CMU Mock Trial aims to better educate its members about the U.S. legal system and trial advocacy. Members gain opportunities to travel, participate in collegiate mock trial tournaments, and compete in the regional competition.

name: Moneythink CMU
description: Moneythink is an American educational non-profit organization that aims to increase the financial capability of American youth by training college volunteers to serve as financial mentors for low-income high school students. Our mission is built on the promise of a prosperous, inclusive, and equitable society for all.

name: Mortarboard
description: Mortar Board, a national honor society that recognizes college seniors for their achievements in scholarship, leadership and service, provides opportunities for continued leadership development, promotes service to colleges and universities and encourages lifelong contributions to the global community.

name: MTNA (Music Teachers National Association) Collegiate Chapter at Carnegie Mellon University
Multicultural Greek Council
description: The Multicultural Greek Council at Carnegie Mellon University provides formal recognition and representation for its culturally based fraternities and sororities while serving as both a supportive and legislative body. Grounded in multiculturalism, MGC values diversity in all its forms and creates a platform for members to voice their perspectives and be heard. The Council fosters unity within the Greek community, promotes cultural awareness, encourages scholastic achievement, and ensures cooperation, accountability, and harmony among its member organizations.

name: Music Production Club
description: We seek to foster a vibrant music production community on campus, providing a platform for music producers and enthusiasts of all skill levels and genres to come together. Whether you're a beginner eager to dive into the world of music production or an experienced producer seeking like-minded individuals, our club is your sonic producer haven. We strive to create an inclusive space where members can learn, discuss, and share ideas on diverse facets of music production, including software, hardware, musical concepts, and production techniques. No genre is off-limits, and no digital audio workstation (DAW) is overlooked. Our club embraces the spectrum of musical possibilities, encouraging collaboration and exploration. We host a variety of production-related activities that are tailored to newcomers, as well as discussions on anything and everything related to music production in today’s music scene.

name: Muslim Student Association
description: The Muslim Student Association at CMU strives to address the religious, cultural, and social needs of Muslim students, and to build awareness and familiarity with Islam, Islamic cultures, and Muslims through positive interactions with the broader CMU community.

name: National Organization of Minority Architects Students
description: The National Organization of Minority Architects (NOMA) mission is to champion diversity within the design professions by promoting the excellence, community engagement, and professional development of its members.It is important to emphasize not only diversity of appearance, but also diversity of thoughts, views, and opinions.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description: Founded in 1979 and chartered in 1980, NSBE's mission is "to increase the number of culturally responsible Black Engineers whoexcel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community."

name: National Society of Black Engineers - Healthcare Innovation Special Interest Group Chapter
description: CMU NSBE HISIG mission to support, promote, and develop Black engineers, scientists, and entrepreneurs as they create culturally responsible and racially equitable innovations in healthcare, medicine, biotechnology, and allied fields.

name: Net Impact Club
description: Net Impact is a community of more than 50,000 student and professional leaders creating positive social and environmental change in the workplace and the world.

name: Neuroscience Student Advisory Council
description: The purpose of Nu Rho Psi @ CMU is to spread awareness of and education about neuroscience to the CMU campus and the rest of the Pittsburgh Community, while also providing the opportunity for outstanding neuroscience students to be recognized as part of a national honor society.

name: Nexus of Community Engagement
description: The Nexus of Community Engagement is a physical space on campus, located on the first floor of the Cohon University Center, as well as a collective of expertise and resources to support the development and implementation of community engagement initiatives, strategies and impact on campus, in the local community and beyond.Fall 2025 HoursMonday, 8:30 am - 5:00 pmTuesday, 8:30am - 8:00 pmWednesday, 8:30 am - 8:00 pmThursday, 8:30 am - 8:00 pmFriday, 8:30am - 5:00 pmSaturday, ClosedSunday, 4:00pm - 7:00pmWant to reserve the Nexus for your community engagement meeting, discussion or event? Reserve at https://www.cmu.edu/student-affairs/celd/nexus/reserve-the-nexus.html.

name: Office for Institutional Equity and Title IX
description: The Office for Institutional Equity and Title IX is dedicated to promoting equity at Carnegie Mellon University, which includes coordinating the University's efforts to prevent and effectively respond to all forms of discrimination (including gender identity discrimination) and sexual misconduct impacting community members, including sexual assault, sexual exploitation, sexual harassment, dating and domestic violence, and stalking. We support all community members, including undergraduate and graduate students, staff, and faculty, at all of our programs and locations, around the world.

name: Office of Community Engagement and Leadership (CELD)
description: The Office of Community Engagement and Leadership Development is committed to educating and empowering students to become effective community-minded leaders who respond to unmet social needs, advocate with and for their communities, and act to create positive societal change.

name: Office of International Education (OIE)
description: OIE serves CMU's international student and scholar community by advising individuals on immigration, personal, academic, social, and acculturation issues.

name: Office of Student Involvement and Traditions (SIT)
description: VisionAn engaged campus community that embraces Carnegie Mellon’s uniquely metacurricular experiences–fostering pride, connection, and a shared sense of purpose in support of the university’s mission.Our Values in ActionThe Office of Student Involvement and Traditions……amplifies and promotes the value of involvement in the student metacurricular experience by highlighting our student organization and student government communities and their ideas and achievements.…celebrates the uniquely-CMU experience by planning memory-making moments and supporting student-led traditions and events. …practices high-impact advising - prioritizing relationships and strong communication skills to create environments where students can celebrate their successes, practice self-efficacy, fail safely, and be challenged to lead with integrity.…prepares the next generation of leaders through strategic education and opportunities for experiential learning and reflection. …prioritizes access to the benefits of our student organization communities for all and ensures that Carnegie Mellon’s parallel commitments to inclusivity and diversity of perspective are reflected in the experiences of individuals within those communities.…bridges university policies, procedures, and operations with student ideas and creativity and activates our community partnerships to help actualize student goals.…centers advocacy and progress, understanding undergraduate and graduate students’ evolving priorities while leveraging strategic assessment to focus our efforts and address the most pressing needs in our community. …serves the CMU community and strategic partners through our focused expertise, data on the student experience, and vast knowledge of the campus network.

name: TartanConnect Officer Support
description: TartanConnect is CMU's new official platform for student organizations and events. Our mission is to make TartanConnect widely used across campus to foster seamless communication, collaboration, and engagement within the CMU community.

name: The CMU Pantry
description: The CMU Pantry is committed to reducing hunger among students by providing nutritious food at no cost. We are dedicated to serving our student community with accessible food resources to promote a healthier, balanced university experience.

name: Operations & General Management Club
description: The mission of the Operations & General Management Club is to promote understanding of and foster initiatives in production operations, service operations, supply chain management, general management, program management, strategy, operations consulting, and operations research studies and activities.

name: Organizational Leadership Club
description: OLC augments the Leadership experience at Tepper: providing members opportunities to hone their leadership and teamwork skills in professional and social settings through hands-on application of tactics and sharing of real-world experiences.

name: Origami Club
description: The CMU Origami Club seeks to promote the practice and appreciation of paper folding and for the cultural and historical significance of origami. We foster the creation of new and original works of origami, and we examine the applications of origami outside of art.

name: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics
description: oSTEM empowers LGBTQ+ people in STEM to succeed personally, academically, and professionally by cultivating environments and communities that nurture innovation, leadership, and advocacy. At CMU, our oSTEM branch is working to build a robust community of learners dedicated to inclusivity and professional development.

name: Out&Allied
description: 1. Provide a supportive environment to enhance the social, academic, and career experiences of current and prospective LGBT students, allies, and partners at Tepper.2. Establish relationships and foster dialogue with employers and alumni who value a diverse workplace.3. Equip future business leaders to be advocates in the workplace.4. Link the social and professional network of Out&Allied to similar groups from other business schools and the greater community.

name: Out@CMU
description: Our mission is to build a safe and supportive community among LGBTQ+ undergraduate students across all of Carnegie Mellon’s colleges through a professional lens. We aim to build a professional network of driven students and passionate leaders - members of the LGBTQ+ community and allies are welcome

name: PACE - Partners Allied in Community Engagement
description: Partners Allied in Community Engagement (PACE) is part of the community engagement student staff housed in the Community Engagement and Leadership Development Office. PACE is comprised of paid student staff members who coordinate a wide range community engagement activities for students, helping to establish a pervasive culture of community engagement on campus. Community engagement involves a mutually beneficial exchange of knowledge and/or effort between CMU and the community. We center an asset-based mindset, acknowledging and valuing the knowledge and lived experiences of community members we have the opportunity to work alongside. Community engagement efforts, which can include hands-on projects designed to meet community-identified needs, educational opportunities to explore the complexity of social issues, and democratic engagement, PACE strives to positively impact the local community through our programs.  PACE offers:-Serve and Learn projects that directly support the mission of our community partners-Educational programs such as workshops, lunch and learns, guest speakers, book clubs, and more-Consultation and support for CAs and RAs, student orgs and FSL groups to plan community engagement eventsFollow us on Instagram @cmupace

name: Pakistani Student Association
description: Our mission is to unite Pakistani students and the CMU community, sharing the vibrant music and culture of Pakistan. We aim to create a welcoming space for everyone to explore and appreciate Pakistan's rich heritage, ensuring inclusivity and representation for Pakistanis at CMU while fostering cross-cultural connections

name: Panhellenic Council
description: The National Panhellenic Conference (NPC) is the premier advocacy and support organization for the advancement of the sorority experience. NPC was established in 1902 to assist collegiate and alumnae chapters of the NPC member organizations in cooperating with colleges and universities and to foster interfraternal relationships. We are committed to relationships built on trust through transparency, accountability and mutual respect. Innovation and our core values of friendship, leadership, service, knowledge, integrity and community guide us in fulfilling our mission.

name: Partners in Speculative Fiction
description: Partners in Speculative Fiction Book Club seeks to explore different stories, novels, and genres through weekly meetings. This club analyzes a variety of speculative fiction works and holds open discussions through on-campus gatherings.

name: Pastry Pals Baking Club
description: Our mission is to create a welcoming, low-commitment space where students can de-stress, connect, andexplore the joy of baking. Through communal kitchens, themed social nights, and community initiatives,we provide the tools and opportunities for students of all backgrounds to bake, bond, and give back. Weaim to support local charities by hosting bake sales or collaborating with organizations that serveunderprivileged communities, offering both delicious treats and support to those in need.

name: Pennsylvania Collegiate Music Educators Association
description: The mission of this organization is to advance music education by promoting the understanding and making of music by all. This mission is aligned with our national organization, NAfME.

name: Pi Tau Sigma
description: Pi Tau Sigma is the International Mechanical Engineering Honors Society. Our mission is to recognize superior scholarship, provide networking and career opportunities, and encourage kinship within the Mechanical Engineering Community.

name: Pickled Arts
description: Pickled Arts is a space for students of all backgrounds, identities, andexperiences to come together and make art. Every student across Carnegie Mellon University is welcome to explore artistic practices in a low-stakes environment and join a creative community of student artists.

name: Pittsburgh Connections
description: Pittsburgh Connections engages students in outdoor recreation, environmental stewardship, and cultural awareness throughout Pittsburgh and Western Pennsylvania, with the goals to help students develop and maintain a physically active lifestyle, build leadership skills, and increase their appreciation of the region.Throughout the academic year, Pittsburgh Connections sponsors outdoor and cultural adventures to one of the many outdoor recreation facilities in the Pittsburgh area. Past adventure trips include whitewater rafting, kayaking, biking, hiking, paintball, horseback riding, and skiing!

name: Pittsburgh Go Association
description: The Pittsburgh Go Association is a go club located in Pittsburgh, PA dedicated to promoting the game of go (baduk, igo, weiqi) in the form of tournaments, rankings, and educational opportunities.

name: Pre-College Programs
description: Carnegie Mellon’s Pre-College Programs mirror the undergraduate experience, giving rising high school juniors and seniors the opportunity to explore their interests and passions while receiving instruction from acclaimed Carnegie Mellon faculty and staff. We provide a holistic and robust student experience that includes academic and personal development both inside and outside of the classroom to prepare students for the future.

name: Pre-Law Society
description: Our mission is to connect undergraduate students at Carnegie Mellon to resources and opportunities in the legal world that may be of benefit to them, as well as provide a community for students interested in attending law school after CMU.

name: PRISM
description: PRISM is a social and activist organization at Carnegie Mellon University for those of all genders and orientations. We provide a safe space for anyone with an open mind to find new friends, talk about issues, and raise awareness.

name: Product @ CMU
description: To build a community and support system for CMU students and alumni in product management, without barriers of majors and year.

name: Product Lab
description: It shall be the purpose of this organization to help students foster Product Management skills by working on projects with participating clients, including but not limited to Startups, Research Institutes, Corporate, Non-Government, and Non-Profit organizations.

name: Project Ignite
description: To utilize project based learning to expose high school students to subject areas that they would otherwise be unable to explore, and to enable high school students to pursue a new or existing interest that they lack the knowledge, resources, or support to engage in on their own, thus helping them develop passions that can be pursued at a professional level.

name: Project Smile
description: Our mission is to improve the overall well being and happiness of the student body through various acts of kindness motivated by scientific research.

name: Puzzlehunt CMU
description: Puzzle Hunt CMU is an interest group in all things related to puzzle hunts on and around the CMU campus. We have weekly meetings to discuss, write, and solve puzzles. We also run a puzzle hunt every semester.If you join us at our meetings, then you can contribute your own puzzles to the upcoming hunt, or you can playtest other people's puzzles. By coming to meetings, you will be helping us staff the upcoming hunt.If you want to play in the hunt, then stay tuned for a Facebook event, posters around campus, and a date on our website https://puzzlehunt.club.cc.cmu.edu/. Our fall hunt is around November, and our spring hunt is around April.We also hold General Body Meetings which are casual events where any member can come and solve puzzles.Most of our contact happens in Discord.

name: Quant
description: The Quant Club of Carnegie Mellon University focuses on the study of quantitative finance, more specifically areas in finance where the study of mathematics and other computational aspects are emphasized.

name: Quiz Bowl
description: The purpose of CMU Quiz Bowl is to encourage participation in Quiz Bowl among students and those in the Pittsburgh region.

name: Recreational Volleyball Club
description: The mission of this club is to provide a space for members of the CMU community to play volleyball and improve the level of play for anyone and everyone who wants to play the game. All activities and functions of the organization must be legal under University, local, state, and federal laws.

name: Redeemed Campus Fellowship
description: Our mission is to build a community of fearless young people grounded in the principles of the Kingdom of God. We aim to cultivate loving, bold, and Holy Spirit-led leaders who live lives of light, shaped by the transformative power of God’s word. We welcome all students—regardless of background or belief—who are interested in exploring faith, engaging in meaningful community, and growing in purpose and leadership.

name: ReFlexion
description: As one of the few acapella groups dedicated to community outreach at CMU, ReFlexion’s goal is to spread our love of music not only to people on campus, but also to the surrounding Pittsburgh areas by performing at senior centers, children’s hospitals, and nearby schools. Our music covers a wide variety of genres, inspired by our group’s diverse music tastes, and we hope to inspire and spread positivity through our performances.

name: Rhythm Game Club @CMU
description: Our mission is to have a place for students to gather around our common love for rhythm games and to spread awareness of these games to the broader student population. As opposed to the current CMU Esports club, our club is a lot more focused on students who may not be interested in shooters and fighting games, giving a chance for both casual gamers and hardcore gamers to come together with a more relaxing genre of game that combines music and unique gameplay.

name: Robotics Club
description: The Carnegie Mellon Robotics Club, also known as "Roboclub", is a student organization at Carnegie Mellon University. At the heart of our mission we are a makerspace & social space. Members use our space and tools to work on all sorts of robotics projects for classes, club projects, or just general curiosity. We were founded in 1984 which makes us one of the oldest collegiate robotics clubs in the world.We're located in the Cohon University Center Rm LL89 (past the mail room and PNC bank, next to Scotch'n'Soda).

name: Roller Hockey Club
description: To allow all students to participate in the sport of roller hockey.

name: Roosevelt at CMU
description: Roosevelt @CMU is a nonpartisan student led policy based think tank, providing students with a national network and resources to develop and implement policy ideas at the campus, local, state, and national levels.

name: Rugby Football Club
description: CMU Rugby's mission is to encourage physical, mental and social wellbeing for both undergraduate and graduate students through the game of Rugby. As a club we bring together members of all backgrounds and abilities and to teach them how to play rugby, and to build a tight network of friends on and off the field. We practice twice a week for most of the academic year and we compete in both the Spring and the Fall.

name: Schatz Club
description: Quantity over Quality

name: Scholars of Finance
description: At Scholars of Finance, our nation-wide mission statement is to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow. Within our branch at Carnegie Mellon, we strive to develop those traits within every member of our organization through large symposiums, speaker series, professional mentorship, and value forums. In doing so, we endeavor to build a future in which the finance leaders of tomorrow steward the world's capital to serve the greater good.

name: School of Music Student Advisory Board
description: The School of Music Student Advisory Board is a group of students committed to improving the community and experience within the Music School. We hear student's ideas and concerns and take on projects to better the school.

name: Scotch'n'Soda Theatre
description: As one of the nation's oldest student theatre organizations, Carnegie Mellon University's Scotch'n'Soda Theatre is an entirely student-run group dedicated to the development of theatrical works and educating the campus community on all aspects of theatre.Scotch'n'Soda is an interdisciplinary organization that values a versatile and diverse constituency devoted to creating unique, innovative, and entertaining products. In collaborating on these products, Scotch'n'Soda builds a strong social network both on campus and off, fostering a safe, supportive environment for creative exploration and artistic expression. Ticket prices are kept low or are free of charge in order to encourage student patronage, and Scotch'n'Soda strives to make its shows accessible to all audience members while simultaneously challenging them to discover exciting new works and revisiting old favorites.Most importantly, from directing to hanging lighting to acting, painting, and even writing, there are students in every position of every show.In addition, S'n'S has three subtroupes: The No Parking Players, Tisbert Sketch Comedy, and New Works Coffeehouse. If you're interested in improv, sketch comedy, or writing original work, check them out!Also, feel free to check us out at our website! (www.snstheatre.org/)Contact Email: snstheatre.president@gmail.com

name: Scottie Sound Bath
description: Scottie Sound Bath is a pioneering endeavor in wellness and innovation, driven by the passionate minds of music entrepreneurship students at the School of Music in collaboration with Student Affairs and Athletics, Physical Education and Recreation. Our mission is to introduce the transformative power of sound baths to the university community and to facilitate further scientific investigation into the healing power of music. Our goal is to add an enjoyable research-based modality to CMU’s menu of self-care offerings.

name: Scottie Ventures
description: Scottie Ventures' mission is to introduce Carnegie Mellon students to the world of venture capital and private equity by giving them the opportunity to develop their skills, knowledge, and network needed to be successful in the industry.

name: Scotty Survivor
description: Scotty Survivor is an organization that provides students the opportunity to play and/or produce acollegiate version of CBS Survivor. We aim to be a social club that connects students from all different years and majors, while allowing them to compete in physical and mental challenges.

name: ScottyCon
description: ScottyCon is a student organization dedicated to planning and running ScottyCon, an annual anime and gaming convention catered to CMU and the greater Pittsburgh community. ScottyCon aims to connect the different subcultures of CMU by creating a collaborative event between different clubs at CMU. ScottyCon strives to continuously grow with its collaborations and integrate various organizations with each iteration. Visit our website for more info: https://www.scottycon.com/

name: ScottyLabs
description: ScottyLabs fosters tech innovation through software development, hackathons, and educational workshops. The group builds and maintains widely used campus applications like CMU Courses to improve student life. They also host TartanHacks, the university's largest annual hackathon, alongside a student-led startup accelerator called Foundry.

name: Second Language Acquisition Networking Group
description: CMU has a long history of an interest in creating a globally prepared student body. This goal is a necessary one as CMU students go on to leadership positions in industries around the world. Similarly, CMU is full of speakers/learners of the wide variety of the world's languages. However, English is nearly always the lingua franca of most work at CMU. Our organization hopes to act as a place where those interested in using/practicing a heritage/native/second language or understanding how languages can be better learned can meet. This will  provide an opportunity for those interested to have a chance to use languages other than English and learn about how language learning works. In this way, our organization -at its heart- aims to practice inclusivity that allows students to learn from each other and network to gain access to opportunities.

name: SEED Leadership Program
description: The mission of the SEED (Sustainability Education + Empowerment = Doing) Leadership program is to build a network of sustainability-literate student leaders capable of empowering and educating peers about sustainability issues.

name: Shotokan Karate Club
description: The mission of CMU Shotokan Karate is to provide training opportunities for students learning karate-do. Karate is a martial art, or fighting method, involving a variety of techniques, including blocks, strikes, evasions, throws, and joint manipulations. The word karate is a combination of two Japanese characters: kara, meaning empty, and te, meaning hand. Thus, karate means "empty hand." Adding the suffix "-do", meaning "way," i.e., karate-do, implies karate as a total way of life that goes well beyond the self-defense applications.In traditional karate-do, we always keep in mind that the true opponent is oneself. The club has existed at Carnegie Mellon since 1966. Our objectives are to:1) provide excellent karate instruction to the community;2) encourage students to make progress through continuous testing;3) produce new generation of black-belt karate instructors.Our senior dojo leader (Bruce Schmidt) has been training at CMU since 1975. We intend to continue this rich tradition of karate training at Carnegie Mellon.

name: Sigma Eta Pi
description: To provide real-world experience to budding entrepreneurs through education and social opportunities that will foster the next generation of entrepreneurs at CMU and provide lifelong connections to members.

name: Sigma Tau Delta International English Honor Society
Singapore Students' Association
description: The Singapore Students' Association at Carnegie Mellon University is a student organization dedicated to fostering an understanding of Singapore culture among the CMU community, as well as promoting interaction among its members. It also serves as a support network for incoming Singaporean students.

name: Ski & Snowboard Club
description: To provide students the opportunity to ski and snowboard during the season while at CMU, and to provide students with a community of those who also share their love for skiing/snowboarding.

name: SKY@CMU - Meditation Club
description: Bringing meditation, breathwork, emotional intelligence, social connection, and service leadership to college students, faculty and staff.National Chapter: https://www.skycampushappiness.org/about

name: Slackline Club
description: Our mission is to build and grow the slacklining community at CMU by providing access to gear and friends to practice slacklining with. We strive to cultivate a hands on community to learn and practice the art of slackening, from beginners to experts.

name: Smart Woman Securities
description: Smart Woman Securities is a non-profit organization dedicated to educating women about the world of finance. We accomplish this task through instructive seminars, mentoring initiatives, and meetings with successful investors such as Warren Buffett. SWS is one of the few female-only investment organizations featuring undergraduate women in the United States. Our mission is to endow every woman with the proper skills and training in order to successfully invest on her own. Currently, there are 25 chapters operating at various universities in the United States. At our chapter, we have taken the initiative in trying to engage women about the various fields within finance. We try to open females' eyes to the endless opportunities that are within the finance arena while giving them the confidence to succeed.

name: Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics Student Chapter
description: A student chapter of the international professional organization SIAM, that is focused on current mathematical research and its applications. The SIAM student chapter hosts seminars and working groups to provide an opportunity for students to learn more about a variety of topics on applied mathematics.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers
description: SASE is dedicated to the advancement of Asian heritage scientists and engineers in education and employment so that they can achieve their full career potential. In addition to professional development, SASE also encourages members to contribute to the enhancement of the communities in which they live.Our mission is to:Prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world.Celebrate diversity on campuses and in the workplace.Provide opportunities for members to make contributions to their local communities.SASE membership is open to men and women of all ethnic backgrounds.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
description: SHPE CMU empowers the Latinx community at CMU and the university community by providing academic, professional, social, and community outreach events which help our members grow and connect. We are more than just our Latinx or STEM affiliations, so we encourage inclusion and collaborations with other organizations and search for ways to grow the voices of ourmembers.

name: Society of Physics Students
description: The Society of Physics Students (SPS) aims to engage all members of the Carnegie Mellon University Physics Department through weekly meetings and physics-themed events. It particularly focuses on undergraduate physics majors, promoting excitement about physics as well as personal and professional growth. The organization’s activities offer opportunities to socialize and to learn more about the paths available to them as physics students.This organization is established as a local chapter of the National Society of Physics StudentsChapter # 991 within Zone 7.This organization also manages the local Sigma Pi Sigma physics honor society chapter # 483The name of this organization shall be "The Carnegie Mellon University Society of Physics Students," abbreviated "CMU SPS."

name: Society of Women Engineers
description: To promote the image and success of women in STEM both internal and external to SWE by providing resources for personal and professional development and a community that advocates diversity and awareness.

name: Soundbytes
description: Formed in 1996, Soundbytes is a group that strives to put the smooth and sultry into their performances, whether it's singing together on a whim or competing on the big stage. Prioritizing vibes and musicality equally, they're a group of close friends who enjoy making music together. In 2023, they competed in Troy, NY for ICCA and brought back awards for best arrangement, best soloist, best choreography, and placed second.

name: South Asian Business Association (SABA)
description: -Provide an interface for students coming to the US for the first time (in terms of a welcome package with reference to life in Pittsburgh, items they should bring with them, what to expect in Pittsburgh, housing tips, etc).-Institute a customized mentoring program within the SABA. Mentors will aim to provide insights into the classes at Tepper, life at Tepper, opportunities for international students and issues within Tepper that new students need to be aware of.-Help members with their job search - we aim to prepare a database of South Asian Tepper alum (with their prior permission) and to use this database to help students learn more about companies they target in their career search. This is not a substitute for the COC - we believe that South Asian students will find it easier to informally speak with South Asian alumni (especially those from their respective countries), and similarly, alum would be open to help students from their countries.-Bring in guest speakers to talk to the SABA about opportunities for international students (specifically, from South Asia). This will not conflict with the COC's recruiting activities.-Hold cultural events on festivals like Diwali or Independence Day and welcome / farewell parties.-We are in informal discussions with similar associations in other business schools (Wharton and Columbia at this point of time) to co-ordinate and hold common activities and events. Eventually, we hope to link a number of these organizations across several business schools.

name: Spanish And Latin Student Association
description: The Spanish and Latine Student Association (SALSA) is a student-led multicultural organization whose mission is to create an inclusive and impactful community for Latine students at Carnegie Mellon University. SALSA aims to do this by hosting social and educational events that highlight the diverse range of cultures that Latines share as well as the issues that impact us. SALSA also works to strengthen connection, intersectionality, and solidarity among other multicultural organizations, emphasizing the importance of recognizing that Latines do not have a standard look or identity.

name: Spikeball Club
description: Spikeball/roundnet is a fun, exciting recreational sport with a devoted and passionate community. Our goal is to create a friendly and welcoming space for spikeball enthusiasts of all skill levels to enjoy the wonderful sport.

name: SPIRIT
description: Our mission is to serve as a haven for Black students and their allies within the Carnegie Mellon community and to champion the issues and awareness of the Black community and all of its intersections.

name: Spring Carnival Committee
description: To plan an awesome, enjoyable, inclusive, and safe Carnival experience for CMU students, alumni, and the entire Tartan community.

name: Steel City Shershaah
description: The purpose of this organization is to develop and promote a space of artistic and creative growth for South Asian performance, education, and civic engagement. Steel City Shershaah aims to diversify dance with a variety of dance styles including Bollywood, Bhangra, Hip-Hop, etc; and to share South Asian culture.

name: Steel Syncopation
description: The Steel Syncopation is an organization committed to celebrating and performing tap dance. Our mission is to welcome students of all backgrounds and levels to start or continue their learning of tap dance and be able to express their creativity through rhythm. This organization hopes to teach the community how to tap dance and increase the awareness of tap dance. This club aims to create a community of tappers who can come together to perform. This organization hopes to be a stress relief for CMU students, by providing a mentally stimulating and satisfying physical activity. Moreover, we hope to foster the creativity of CMU students by giving them the opportunity to create their own dances and express themselves through rhythmic patterns.

name: Stock Talks
description: Stock Talks is a discussion platform for the conversation and analysis of the stock market. Stock Talks aims to open discussion of the stock market to the community, while teaching students how to use their interests and skills in searching for internships and full-time work.

name: Strong Women Strong Girls
description: Our mission is to empower girls to imagine a broader future through a curriculum grounded on female role models. Our core values are accountability, quality, respect, community, and discovery. As a college chapter, we hope to make an impact on our local Pittsburgh community through mentoring, while also empowering and supporting our college mentors.

name: Student College
description: The Student College was established in 2001 to provide Carnegie Mellon students with the opportunity to share knowledge through educational, self-designed courses. Students can teach classes on any topic of their choice. However, the course cannot be available through regular University offerings. Courses typically meet once a week (for a full semester) and follow the current Carnegie Mellon academic calendar.

name: Student Dormitory Council (SDC)
description: The Student Dormitory Council (SDC) aims to improve the lives of residents at CMU by providing opportunities for residents to participate in events and activities through SDC, learn about residents around campus, and receive funding for their own events!

name: Student Government Director of Finance & JFC
description: The Student Government Director of Finance oversees the student activities fee including the allocation of funds to student organizations at Carnegie Mellon. They advocate for student organizations on financial matters, answer questions and problem solve related to the use of activities fee money, and oversee the process used to distribute and reconcile fees each year.  The Joint Funding Committee (JFC) is the body that supports the budget request and allocation processes.

name: Student Government Director of Organizations & CoSO
description: The Student Government Director of Organizations oversees and advocates for student organizations at Carnegie Mellon. They direct the recognition of new organizations, re-recognition of existing organizations, and allocation/management of campus space for student organizations.  The Committee on Student Organizations (CoSO) and University Center Allocation Board (UCAB) are the bodies that support these processes.

name: Student Government Executive Branch
description: The Carnegie Mellon Student Government consists of many groups, at their core governed by the Student Body Constitution. The Legislative branch consists of the Undergraduate Student Senate and the Graduate Student Assembly. The Executive branch consists of undergraduate and graduate students who work with campus advocacy, student organizations, and student finances. The Activities Board (AB) is an external committee that provides events, concerts, films, lectures, technical assistance, and other activities for the campus. The Student Dormitory Council (SDC) is composed of student representatives from campus housing and is engaged in improving the quality of student life for residents.

name: Students Using Data for Social-Good
description: We aim to connect socially conscious students from all CMU schools to non-profits where they can apply their technical and communication skills to meaningful data management and analysis projects.

name: Sustainable Earth
description: Sustainable Earth aims to encourage Carnegie Mellon University and the Pittsburgh Community to actively practice ways of living sustainably.

name: Sweepstakes
description: Sweepstakes is responsible for the organizing, conducting, and supervising all activities related to the Sweepstakes (Buggy) competitions.

name: Taiwanese Scholar Society
description: Help incoming Taiwanese students to adapt to life in the US. Connect the Taiwanese community at CMU.Promote Taiwanese culture and community to CMU.

name: Taiwanese Students Association
description: The Taiwanese Students Association at Carnegie Mellon University is an organization that serves to bring cultural awareness, provide service to the community, and create social events throughout the CMU community via cultural, philanthropic, and social events. TSA is non-exclusive and is open to anyone who wishes to join.├ó┬Å┬Á Culture events: Annual Culture Night, monthly Asian Awareness discussions, food festivals, hot pot dinners, and more!├ó┬Å┬Á Social events: Booth, Holiday parties, End-of-The-Year banquet, general meetings, Game Nights, and Late Night dinners!├ó┬Å┬Á Service projects: 1000Plus, volunteer work, weekly food fundraisers!

name: Tartan Athlete Fellowship
description: The Carnegie Mellon Tartan Athlete Fellowship is a student group looking to explore the role athletics play in our Christian faith and how our Christian faith affects the way we compete, play and live. This group uses athletics to facilitate discussions, but not everything is focused on sports. Everyone is welcome to attend, athlete and non-athlete alike. Come check us out if you're interested in being part of a group exploring faith, college, sports and living.Tartan Athlete Fellowship is a ministry of the CCO.Insta: @ccotaf

name: Tartan Cultural League
description: The Tartan Cultural League at Carnegie Mellon University is dedicated to fostering strong connections among Asian student groups through the unifying power of sports. Our mission is to create a vibrant, inclusive community that celebrates the rich diversity of Asian cultures. By organizing and participating in various sports events, we aim to enhance intercultural understanding, promote physical wellness, and build lasting bonds among students from diverse backgrounds.

name: Tartan Cycling
description: Tartan Cycling is established to provide a framework for existing CMU cyclists to connect, as well as a mechanism to introduce interested students to the sport of cycling. We hold group meetings to provide relevant information to new members, organize cycling events accessible to all skill levels, and give students interested in collegiate racing the opportunity to participate.

name: Tartan Links Golf Club
description: Tartan Links is a golf club dedicated to making golf more accessible and affordable for all students at Carnegie Mellon University. Our mission is to lower the barriers to the sport by providing clubs, organizing rides to local golf courses, and creating opportunities for students of all skill levels to play and learn together. Whether you are an experienced golfer or trying the game for the first time, Tartan Links provides the support and community to help you get involved.More than just a golf club, Tartan Links is also a social and professional network. Our name reflects both our connection to the game of golf and our goal of building meaningful "links" within the student community. We aim to create an inclusive environment where students can connect, relax, and grow together. Anyone interested in golfing, meeting new people, or simply being part of the community is welcome to join!

name: Tartan Marketing Association
description: The American Marketing Association at CMU aims to provide opportunities for students to: - Participate in hands-on projects to gain technical and soft skills - Network with professionals and explore possible careers in the marketing and business fields - Develop interests in marketing and the American Marketing Association

name: Tartan NeuroTech
description: Our mission is to engage more students in different areas of study in the field of neurotechnology, more specifically, Brain/Muscle-Computer Interfaces. We achieve our mission through giving student-led intro lectures, organizing industrial talks, and leading hackathon group projects.

name: Tartan Salsa
description: We are a group of Carnegie Mellon students passionate about Latin dance. We are a club for students, run by students. The majority of our members are graduate students, but we also have many undergrads, postdocs, and other Pittsburgh university members in our club!

name: Tartan Time Travelers
description: To discuss and connect ALL students to the importance and essence of history in our lives, fostering a deep appreciation of historical events, figures, and movements that have shaped our world today.

name: Tartan West Coast Swing
description: The mission of Tartan WCS is to promote the art, enjoyment, and community of West Coast Swing (WCS) dancing within Carnegie Mellon University. This will be accomplished by providing regularlessons and socials for the community as well as providing a space for competitive WCS dancers to grow their skills.

name: Tartan Wind Ensemble
description: The mission of the Tartan Wind Ensemble is to provide a place on campus where students and other affiliates of CMU can create a community to rehearse and perform music in a casual ensemble setting.

name: TartanAUV
description: TartanAUV's mission is simple; we build a team of effective engineers, leaders, andproblem-solvers, a strong reputation for excellence, and an (even more notable)AUV.

name: TartanConnect Officer Support
description: TartanConnect is CMU's new official platform for student organizations and events. Our mission is to make TartanConnect widely used across campus to foster seamless communication, collaboration, and engagement within the CMU community.

name: Tau Beta Pi
description: TBP Membership by Invitation OnlyMission from the TBP National Website: As the only academic honor society that confers recognition on eligible individuals from all engineering disciplines, Tau Beta Pi:- Recognizes those who have conferred honor upon their Alma Maters by distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as students in engineering or by their attainments as alumni in the field of engineering;- Provides leadership and professional development training for engineering students and alumni members;- Promotes lifelong alumnus member involvement

name: Tea Appreciation Society
description: The CMU Tea Appreciation Society aims to support and develop tea-drinking through themed sessions featuring teas from countries and cultures around the world. Together, we celebrate diversity through tea.

name: Tech4Society
