description: We work closely with local organizations doing social good by volunteering technical skills to address their needs. We inspire members of the CMU community to enact local change and provide a platform for them to get actively involved in social projects outside of CMU.

name: TEDxCMU
description: TEDxCMU is an entirely student-run, independently-organized TEDx organization that puts on multiple conferences and events each year. We bring together some of the most creative and innovative thinkers to deliver TEDx talks in the Carnegie Mellon and Pittsburgh communities.

name: Tepper A.I. Club
description: Connecting CMU students with the real use of artificial intelligence in business through workshops, skill-shares, and applied projects.The Tepper AI Club helps MBA and master’s students learn how to use AI to do their jobs better—whether that’s in consulting, product management, finance, marketing, or operations. Backed by the Center for Intelligent Business (CIB), the club closes the gap between theory and practice through hands-on learning, real workflows, and guidance from faculty and industry professionals.

name: Tepper Capital Management
description: Tepper Capital Management, formerly known as the Alpha Asset Management Club, is the only student organization at the Tepper School of Business at Carnegie Mellon University that is dedicated exclusively to investment management. We serve the needs of students who are interested in pursuing careers in the field of asset management, as well as others in the Tepper community who are simply interested in becoming better informed investors.We prepare students for careers in fields such as equity research, credit research, hedge funds, and portfolio management. Part of this involves competing in investment competitions against other top MBA programs, and we prepare and send teams to those. There is an opportunity for real life investment management experience through the Tepper Opportunity Fund that is managed through the club. We also meet regularly to discuss investments and market topics, relevant regardless of if you plan on going into asset management as a career. To supplement this we also hold personal finance events and information sessions to make sure all students are prepared to manage their own assets throughout life.Former Tepper Capital Management members have gone on to successful careers at hedge funds firms, mutual fund shops, private wealth managers, and other institutional asset managers. Regardless of what you plan to go into we think there is a lot to be gained by membership. We have a tight knit alumni network that is constantly looking to advance opportunities to members, during their time at Tepper and beyond.

name: Tepper Finance Group
description: The Tepper Finance Group (TFG) aims to foster a collaborative community for CMU students interested in finance. With five clubs each containing a wealth of resources and experience, TFG boasts unrivalled resources on campus for helping students break into the most competitive finance careers.

name: Tepper Games
description: Provide a friendly and open environment where students can socialize and bond over games.

name: Tepper Golf Club
description: In the world of business the ability to confidently approach a round of golf can prove to be quite valuable as a networking tool. To that end, the Tepper Golf Club has three main aims:1) To educate new players about the game play & etiquette.2) To provide additional opportunities for students to golf.3) To organize activities that will allow members to network with fellow students, alumni, faculty and employers.

name: Tepper Outdoor Adventure Club
description: Pittsburgh is easily one of the greenest cities in the country.  It's incredible that places as vast and beautiful as Schenley and Frick Park exist and provide recreational opportunities within Pittsburgh zip codes. And yet, the all consuming rigors of business school can make it difficult to find opportunities to experience nature. Tepper Outdoor Adventure Club seeks to provide such events and to stoke your enthusiasm to get out there!We intend to provide Tepper and CMU students opportunities to get in touch with the outdoors through hiking, cycling, climbing, swimming, running, or other activities. Our goal with these endeavors is to help our members pursue physical fitness goals, develop relationships, and immerse in the beauty of Pittsburgh and its surrounding areas.

name: Tepper Parents Club
description: The Tepper Parents Club, created in 2016, is an inclusive group for families of Tepper students. Our aim is to help incoming and current families create a support system of others who understand the unique challenges faced in MBA school. We hope to help you get involved in the Tepper community as well as adjust to life with a little in Pittsburgh.

name: Tepper Partners Club
description: As a community, the Tepper Partners Club provides inclusive support and tailoredresources that enable partners and their students to thrive in their lives before, during,and after the Tepper experience.

name: Tepper Real Estate Club
description: We aim to provide informative and educational resources, workshops, and activities to promote knowledge of commercial and residential real estate, highlight career and entrepreneurship opportunities across sectors, and provide opportunity to engage and learn about real estate to facilitate growth for those interested in real estate whether professionally or personally.

name: Tepper Undergraduate Real Estate Club
description: TUREC seeks to prepare students for investment and careers in real estate by developing a practical understanding of real estate finance and providing opportunities to build networks in the industry.

name: Tepper Veterans Association
description: The mission of the Tepper Veterans Association is to support transitioning service members and veterans, both U.S. and international, who are interested in pursuing an MBA. Our members are honored to be part of the Tepper community and maintain the camaraderie and esprit de corps in business school that we bring from our military experience. We take great pride in our service and continue to serve by giving back to the past, present, and future Tepper MBA community.

name: Tepper Wine Club
description: Form lasting relationships over the joy of wine tasting events with a nose of education for real life business situations where a limited knowledge of wine can go a long way.

name: Tepper Women in Business Club (TWIB)
description: Development: Support all genders in their development as confident, successful, and inclusive leaders.     Equity: Promote gender equity in the Tepper community and beyond through programming in collaboration with Allies for Gender Equity.     Thrive: Create a supportive environment in which women and allies can thrive through programming on professional development, networking, and educational workshops.     Community: Strengthen and grow the network of Tepper Women in Business, connecting past, present and future.

name: TepperCARES
description: TepperCares leads community service initiatives in the Greater Pittsburgh Area.  Our goal is to provide volunteers with high-quality service experiences that complement the MBA education.

name: Thai Students Association
description: Extend aid for Thai students coming to CMUPromote Thai culture to CMU and the Pittsburgh AreaEstablish Connections with other Thai students in the United StatesRender Assistance with Thai-related matters to the Carnegie Mellon AdministrationRender assistance to Carnegie Mellon ThailandConnect Carnegie Mellon students to Thai companies and other Thai organizations

name: The American Society for Artificial Internal Organs - CMU Chapter
description: The CMU Local Student Chapter of the American Society for Artificial Internal Organs (ASAIO) serves as a university hub connecting students with professional networks in artificial organ technologies and medical device design. It supports career development, research, and leadership in biotech through activities like journal clubs, the ASAIO Student Design Competition, and outreach programs.

name: The Atlas Project
description: Atlas is an interdisciplinary team whose aim is to develop an autonomous buggy that can steer itself through the entirety of the buggy course and compete in the rising robotic buggy competition.

name: The CMU Pantry
description: The CMU Pantry is committed to reducing hunger among students by providing nutritious food at no cost. We are dedicated to serving our student community with accessible food resources to promote a healthier, balanced university experience.

name: The Council for Global Health Scholars
THE FAIR
description: The FAIR showcases many of CMU's 300+ Student Government recognized student organizations, 23 sorority and fraternity organizations, and partners from around campus and the Pittsburgh community. The entire CMU community - undergraduate and graduate students, staff, and faculty - are invited to explore ways to get involved at Carnegie Mellon and beyond.

name: The Flying Club
description: The Flying Club at Carnegie Mellon University is a platform to promote flying and aviation at Carnegie Mellon University and the Greater Pittsburgh area. It is intended to be a space at Carnegie Mellon University where those interested in flying and aviation can congregate, share experiences, promote flying, and help others reach their goals in aviation and flying.

name: The Frame
description: The Frame is a non-profit, student-run art space.  We are committed to showcasing the artistic work of Carnegie Mellon students and to promoting ambitious and experimental art within our community, campus and Pittsburgh.

name: The Oakland Review
description: To publish a literary journal based in the Carnegie Mellon campuscommunity. To cultivate an appreciation and enthusiasm for literature and art in theCarnegie Mellon community.

name: The Originals
description: The purpose of The Originals is to advance musicianship, foster creative expression and push the boundaries of a cappella, both internally, and in the Carnegie Mellon University campus at large.

name: The Tartan
description: The Tartan is a student-run weekly newspaper serving the Carnegie Mellon University community, a student organization dedicated to informing students about affairs and opinions relevant to them, and a forum for opinions from the university community.Members of The Tartan meet weekly on Mondays in UC 314 at 5 p.m. to discuss content for the upcoming week for our various sections, including News, Forum, SciTech, Sports, and Pillbox (The Tartan's Art & Culture magazine). We are always looking for writers, artists, photographers, and copy editors of any skill level! Interested in joining? Fill out this form: https://tinyurl.com/Tartan2022-23Consider joining our Slack for meeting information: https://tinyurl.com/Tarties

name: The Thrifty Mellon
description: We want to create CMU's first on-campus, student run thrift store where students can donate and find clothing, dorm supplies, electronics, etc.

name: The Treblemakers
description: The mission of The Treblemakers is to provide a supportive community for students with a passion for singing and a cappella.

name: The Writing Academy
description: To create a place at CMU for writers to pursue professional and creative interest

name: Theme Park Engineering Group
description: Our organization's mission is to connect people to the themed entertainment industry through opportunities to hear from industry professionals, go to the parks, and network with people that are already involved in the industry. Some overall objectives are to cultivate a community of people that are passionate about themed entertainment, seek university support for people to go to industry conventions, and encourage involvement in industry competitions.

name: Traders at Carnegie Mellon
description: Traders at CMU is a student organization committed to uncovering the black box of quantitative finance.Traders at CMU aims to teach and to trade; our goal is to provide an environment for students to pursue their passion for trading, hone their skills, and apply their ideas in different markets. We believe in learning by doing—through structured education sessions, different trading teams, and competitions around campus, our traders gain firsthand experience in algorithmic trading, market structure and dynamics, and quantitative analysis.

name: Transfer Student Organization
description: Our mission, as the Transfer Student Organization, is to help transfer students maximize their college experience through social, logistical, and academic support, as well as help transfers feel at home here at CMU so they can succeed in all their goals.

name: Turkish Student Society
description: It aims to promote Turkish Culture on Campus and in Pittsburgh, to help new students to adapt the life in Pittsburgh, and to improve the connections between Turkish CMU Alumni and CMU students for networking purposes.

name: Undergraduate Consulting Club
description: We strive to expand the horizons and the possibilities of what our school and our students can do in the consulting world. We provide the necessary tools to support our members in their journey towards a fascinating career in the consulting industry.

name: Undergraduate Entrepreneurship Association
description: CMU Undergrad Entrepreneurship Association is a rapidly growing and inclusive club that fosters a community of aspiring entrepreneurs. Our mission is to provide a platform for students to develop their entrepreneurial skills, connect with industry professionals, and create meaningful relationships with like-minded individuals. We empower our members through a diverse range of events, such as workshops, speaker events, networking opportunities, and competitions.

name: Undergraduate Student Senate
description: The Carnegie Mellon Undergraduate Student Senate is the undergraduate component of Carnegie Mellon's Student Government Legislative Branch.  We are composed of student-elected representatives that represent the unique and diverse culture of our university. Elected representatives from each Carnegie Mellon college meet on a weekly basis to discuss rising issues on our campus.Our general body meetings are every Thursday at 5:00p EST, and are open to everyone in the Carnegie Mellon community. For the 2022-23 school year, GBMs will be held in the Cohon Center Danforth Conference Room.

name: Underground Writer's Society
description: The mission of Underground Writer’s Society is to foster the development of individual writing projects and work with peers to improve story ideas and writing.

name: University Rowing Club
description: CMU's Rowing Team was founded in 1989, and has since become the largest Club sport at Carnegie Mellon. Committed to teamwork, athleticism, and academic excellence, Tartan Crew continues to create an all-inclusive environment for dedicated Student-Athletes.

name: User Experience Student Association
description: UXA mission is to support our members confidence and self-actualization in practicing HCI through fulfilling three fundamental pillars: foster social engagement; provide support for academic success; and accelerate career readiness.

name: Vermillion Anime Club
description: Our mission at Vermillion Anime Club is to foster interest in Japanese anime and manga, and to provide a community for individuals that hold such interests.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description: Our mission as VSA is to promote, preserve, and advocate for the Vietnamese and Vietnamese-American culture. We also hope to aid the growth and development of the Carnegie Mellon University collegiate students through the creation of cultural, advocacy, academic, and community-focused events about the Vietnamese and Vietnamese-American culture.

name: Witchcraft@CMU
description: We are dedicated to exploring magical practice, mysticism, and earth-based spirituality throughrespectful discussion, educational workshops, and inclusive ritual.

name: Women in Biomedical Engineering
description: The mission of CMU Women in BME is to bring knowledge, networking opportunities, and mentorship to women in Biomedical Engineering (BME). Our efforts are aimed at closing the gender gap by increasing the number of women who i) enter the field, ii) pursue higher education, and iii) obtain positions of elevated rank within companies and universities. Our organization is open to individuals of all genders. Members include graduate students, individuals working in industry, and professors interested in discussing the impact of gender in BME and closely-related disciplines.

name: Women in Business
description: Women in Business is an undergraduate organization focused on empowering women in the workplace through raising awareness about gender equity and professional development. We serve to help young women in college navigate the professional field and develop personal connections throughout the community.

name: Women in CyberSecurity
description: WiCyS is where the recruitment, retention and advancement of women in cybersecurity happens. We welcome members of all genders and colleges/programs - as long as you have an interest in cybersecurity, you are welcome here!

name: Women in International Security
description: The mission of CMU WIIS is to elevate the voices of women in the field of international security, and to promote discourse and broaden perspectives regarding the role gender plays in international security through conversations and outreach between students, faculty, and community members.

name: Women in MechE
description: Empowering female engineers who strive to better the world around them while creating a supportive community among students here at CMU. Any member of the Carnegie Mellon University community with interest in the mission and goals of Women in MechE may become a member of the organization. Membership shall not be denied to any person because of age, race, gender, disability, sexual orientation, religion, familial status, or national origin.

name: Women in Product
description: Women in Product is a student organization focused on empowering women in product management through mentorship, hands-on workshops, and peer support. Membership is open to any member of the Carnegie Mellon University community who is interested in the mission and goals of our organization.

name: Women in Science
description: We are the Women in Science Club! We welcome people of any gender, major, and college into our organization. Our club's objective to promote a strong community of support, service, and education between women, other gender minorities, and their peers. We strive to form connections between classes and majors and give women access to mentors and role models within academia and industry. We aim to create a strong community of support for gender minorities in science to navigate our current and future careers through community service, education, professional development, and social events.

name: Women's Club Basketball
description: Our mission is to create an environment for women to compete against each other and play competitive basketball against other schools, while promoting teamwork, sportsmanship, and community.

name: Women's Club Lacrosse
description: Join a community of supportive and energetic women who enjoy the sport of lacrosse. Whether you've played for several years, or you're new to the sport, everyone is welcome and guaranteed to grow while still having fun.

name: Women's Club Soccer
description: CMU's Women's Club Soccer team aims to provide women of Carnegie Mellon University an opportunity to play competitive intercollegiate soccer, and promote fitness, friendship, and teamwork!

name: Women's Club Volleyball
description: Our mission an organization is to provide a fun and safe space for all women to play volleyball at a competitive yet manageable level.

name: Women's Self Defense
description: Our club is dedicated to teaching students the skills needed to effectively protect themselves in situations where they lack a physical advantage. Our aim is to provide students with a foundational understanding of hand-to-hand combat, weapon familiarity, and techniques for defusing hazardous scenarios. Moreover, we'll instruct you on utilizing everyday household items, such as water bottles and umbrellas, as defensive tools in the event of an attack. Our ultimate aspiration is to nurture the abilities of our students to their fullest potential, ensuring their preparedness to navigate and survive potentially harmful or life-threatening encounters. While our curriculum is designed with a focus on women, we warmly welcome individuals of all genders to join us.

name: Women's Ultimate Frisbee Club
description: We are the Money Mellons, CMU's Women's Ultimate Frisbee Club. Our focus is to introduce new players to the sport, as well as advance the growth of skilled players: all levels of experience are welcome (in fact, the majority of players start ultimate in college)! We strive to create a community for people who want to form lifelong friendships and memories and focus on fostering a fun atmosphere that is competitive come game-time.Throughout the school year, we compete in tournaments against other college women's teams from around the country. In the 2023 season, the Mellons qualified for D-I College Nationals and competed among the nation's top teams.If you are interested in playing with us and becoming a part of this community, please reach out to us!

name: Women's Water Polo
description: Women's Water Polo welcomes players of all skill levels and abilities to enjoy the game of water polo. We strive to create a team atmosphere that is fun and supportive, but also competitive during games. Throughout the year, we practice three days a week and compete with other collegiate water polo teams in the region.

name: WRCT 88.3FM
description: As a free-form radio broadcaster, we are committed to providing quality alternatives to the mainstream commercial programming that dominates the radio. Our DJs, Public Affairs hosts, and other staff members are not only Carnegie Mellon students, staff, and faculty, but also community members from the Pittsburgh area, some of whom have more than 30 years of radio broadcasting experience under their belts. We pride ourselves in our substantively diverse programming. We also provide the radio broadcast for the CMU Sweepstakes event, have live bands perform in our studio, and have trained hundreds of CMU students in music production and DJing.

name: Yarnivores
description: Founded in 2012, Yarnivores is an organization that allows knitters and crocheters on campus to meet and discuss projects or learn new skills. We teach yarn crafts, facilitate charitable crafting, and organize yarn crafting events on campus.

name: Young African Leaders Association
description: Our mission is to create a community for the African diaspora on campus by giving a place to talk about and experience their diverse cultures. Furthermore, we hope to spread knowledge about this college to all members of the CMU campus in order to facilitate an environment of inclusion, curiosity and open mindedness.

name: Young Life College
description: Young Life College is all about building authentic community, walking through life together, and creating space to explore faith. Through weekly events, small groups, and trips, students are invited to grow in friendships and take their next steps in following Jesus. It’s a space for everyone — whether you’ve never heard of Jesus or have been walking with Him for a while.
